events.summary: Attack on the USS Kelvin: the Romulan mining vessel Narada arrives from 2387 and engages the USS Kelvin. Captain Richard Robau is ordered aboard the mining ship and is asked the location of Ambassador Spock and the current stardate. When the date is mentioned, Nero immediately kills him in a rage. He resumes his attack, resulting in the destruction of the Kelvin and the death of George Kirk, who took command for twelve minutes after Robau's departure and murder; Winona Kirk gives birth to James T. Kirk on Medical shuttle 37 as it departs the Kelvin.
Erica Ortegas is born in Barranquilla, Colombia on the planet Earth.
James Tiberius Kirk is born to George and Winona Kirk in Riverside, Iowa on the planet Earth.

episodes.title: Where No Man Has Gone Before
In A Mirror, Darkly, Part II
Star Trek IV: The Voyage Home

events.summary: Sylvia Tilly is born.
The Federation survey ship USS Kelvin patrols a region of space approximately 75,000 kilometers from the Federation-Klingon border.
The AP 350 is developed by a Zigwel Orgon.
Una Chin-Riley begins her Starfleet career, and speaks to Neera Ketoul for the last time for twenty-five years.
On Vulcan the young Spock makes a practical joke. In another incident, the young Spock comes home anguished, after being told by other Vulcan children that he was not really Vulcan.

A Klingon terror raid on Doctari Alpha results in the presumed death of Mike and Gabrielle Burnham, and the loss of the experimental Red Angel suit they were working on. Their daughter survives, and Vulcan Ambassador Sarek becomes her legal guardian.
Alpha Lupi goes supernova.
Amanda Grayson and Michael Burnham go to the seventh moon of Eridani D to take part in a book exchange for Michael's tenth birthday.

Following this incident, Burnham decides to run away from Sarek's home. Using the Red Angel suit, her mother appears to Spock from the future, and helps him locate his adopted sister, who is eventually rescued from the clutches of a predatory animal by Sarek at Vulcan's Forge.
May Ahearn is born.

Sometime later, the Vulcan Learning Center is bombed by Vulcan extremists, resulting in Michael Burnham's being physically dead for three minutes. Sarek used the healing powers of his katra to bring her back to life. As a result, the two of them remained telepathically connected for the rest of their lives, with the ability to reach out to the other in moments of distress.
The Anti-Food Replication Movement is active in this year.

The SS Columbia expedition from the American Continent Institute crash lands on the planet Talos IV. Theodore Haskins and his entire party are killed, save one survivor named Vina. Before landing, the ship emits a radio-interference distress call that will be intercepted by the USS Enterprise in 2254.
After playing a practical joke on someone, Spock is branded as a "practical joker" in his hometown of ShiKahr on Vulcan.

In an alternate timeline, Spock is killed by a le-matya. Following his death, his mother Amanda separates from her husband Sarek. Later that year, she is killed in an accident at Lunaport. Subsequent to the death of his wife and son, Sarek represents the Vulcan government as ambassador to seventeen worlds for the next thirty years.
In yet another timeline, Spock lives as well as his pet sehlat I-Chaya.
Nyota Uhura is born in Kenya, Earth.
Spock is tested mentally and physically by the kahs-wan ritual on his home planet of Vulcan.
T'Pring is betrothed to Spock through a mind meld.
The Ba'ul of planet Kaminar become warp capable.
The pet sehlat I-Chaya given to Spock by his father, Sarek, is killed when he defended the young boy from a le-matya.
The USS Enterprise on a return flight via a time warp to the year 2267, passes through this year briefly as the vessel breaks momentum.
Robert Johnson is born.
Brack purchases the planet Holberg 917G.

The USS Archimedes makes first contact with the Ba'ul after a communication is received from the planet. The Ba'ul High Council is found to be hostile, and the message received from a member of a pre-warp subject species, the Kelpiens. The Kelpien is extracted by Lieutenant Philippa Georgiou and given refugee status by the Federation.
Elaine Johnson is born.
Me Hani Ika Hali Ka Po is born.
Another starship disappears near the Taurean system.
Montgomery Scott joins Starfleet and begins his engineering career. Over the next fifty-two years, he serves on a combined total of eleven freighters, cruisers, and starships.
Pavel Chekov is born to Andrei Chekov, four years earlier than in the prime timeline.
Vader, a resident of the Caldos colony, dies on the 23rd of September.
Arlene Galway is born.
Richard Daystrom invents the duotronic computer and is subsequently awarded the Nobel and Zee-Magnees Prizes for his achievements.
The USS Farragut is launched.
One of the Human males examined by Dr. McCoy in the Omicron colony was born in this year.
Straal and Paul Stamets begin working together.

Emony Dax visits Earth to judge a gymnastics competition at the University of Mississippi, where she meets Leonard McCoy. She told him he "had the hands of a surgeon". Afterward, he began his career in medicine.
In an alternate timeline, the Enterprise is launched from the Luna Shipyards on Luna.
Michael Burnham begins attending the Vulcan Science Academy, making her the first Human to do so.

episodes.title: The Butcher's Knife Cares Not for the Lamb's Cry

events.summary: Pavel Chekov is born to Andrei Chekov in Russia, on Earth.
The inconclusive Battle of Donatu V is fought between Federation and Klingon forces near Sherman's Planet.
The USS Cayuga is launched.
The USS Constellation (NCC-1017) is launched under the command of Captain Matthew Decker. The ship begins its first of three five-year exploratory missions.

The USS Enterprise (NCC-1701) is launched from San Francisco Fleet Yards under the command of Captain Robert April. His wife, Sarah April, is the vessel's first medical officer and Christopher Pike is April's first officer.
An exotic fungus destroys most of the planet Tarsus IV's food supply.
Captain Robert April chooses to break the General Order 1 and warn the Perricans about a possible apocalyptic meteor shower that was due to hit their planet.

Doctor Tristan Adams begins to revolutionize and Humanize prisons and the treatment of prisoners by developing new methods to treat the criminally insane. Meanwhile, the Federation begins to convert a number of their penal colonies over to asylums to treat inmates, rather than to "cage" them up.

James T. Kirk – at the age of thirteen – is one of nine surviving eyewitnesses to a massacre of some four thousand colonists on Tarsus IV by Governor Kodos. After supply ships arrive, Earth Forces find a burned body that is assumed to be Kodos, the case is closed. Still alive, Kodos secretly assumes the identity of actor Anton Karidian.
As he claims, this is the last time before 2267, that Montgomery Scott sees a PXK pergium reactor.
Dusty Swender is born in Syracuse, New York, North America.
Lenore Karidian is born.
Muliq Al Alcazar is born in the Tendara Colony.
Oriana is born.
Sylvia Tilly graduates from Musk Junior High School.
T'quiel Dawn is born in North Continent, Cerberus Colony.

The first of many thousands of lives lost in the Eminiar-Vendikar War in a star system of NGC 321 is recorded by the United Federation of Planets. After suffering for nearly two decades this mounting loss of life, the Federation would seek permission from the planet Eminiar VII for the construction of a treaty port in their star system in 2267.
Andrea Lopez is born in Starbase 2.
Captain April recommends that Una Chin-Riley receive the Starfleet Medal of Gallantry for her actions during the Marcel disaster.
Captain Robert April chooses to break the General Order 1 by sharing Federation technology with the inhabitants of Nar'el, so they could avoid an extinction-level drought.
Maat Al-Salah is born on Outpost 4.
Rukiya is born.

episodes.title: Lift Us Where Suffering Cannot Reach

events.summary: Starfleet loses contact with the probe Friendship 1, launched in 2067.
Yuuto Hoshide is born in Misawa, Aomori, Japan.
As a lieutenant, Christopher Pike travels to Majalan system and meets Alora when he rescues her from a shuttle accident.

episodes.title: Lift Us Where Suffering Cannot Reach

events.summary: Charles Evans is born.
Joanna McCoy, daughter of Leonard McCoy, is born.
Me Hani Ika Hali Ka Po builds a universal translator.
Michael Burnham graduates from the Vulcan Science Academy and begins her seven-year service aboard the USS Shenzhou under Captain Philippa Georgiou.

episodes.title: The Butcher's Knife Cares Not for the Lamb's Cry

events.summary: Spock declines his admission to the Vulcan Science Academy due to the council minister referring to his Human mother as a "disadvantage" and enlists in Starfleet.
Sylvia Tilly, at the age of sixteen, went through a "rebellious phase" in her life, and she was involved with hacking.
Tina Lawton is born.
After serving as first officer under Captain April, Christopher Pike receives a promotion to captain and assumes command of the Enterprise.
Captain Matthew Decker finishes his first five-year mission aboard the Constellation.
James T. Kirk is introduced to Gary Mitchell. Years later, they became friends when cadet Mitchell attended Lieutenant Kirk's philosophy class at Starfleet Academy.

episodes.title: Where No Man Has Gone Before

events.summary: Lucille Harewood is born on Earth.
Robert April leaves the USS Enterprise as its commander for an appointment as Federation ambassador-at-large.
Sarek becomes estranged from his son for the next eighteen years.
Spock graduates Starfleet Academy with a commission of ensign after enrolling there instead of the Vulcan Science Academy, against Sarek's wishes.
Una Chin-Riley is made First officer.
Leland conducts a mission to Deneva that unfortunately involves the wrong ambassador.

Leonard McCoy heads a mass-inoculation program on the planet Dramia II. After his force leaves, a massive plague strikes the planet, killing most of its people. The Dramens hold him responsible and nineteen years later he is arrested, with Federation approval, and put on trial for the outbreak.
Charles Evans is the sole survivor of his ship's crash landing on the planet Thasus.

James T. Kirk begins a five-year officer training program at Starfleet Academy. In his first year at the Academy, he develops a romantic relationship with Ruth and is tormented by an older cadet named Finnegan.
June 9th – May Ahearn dies.
Kirk befriends Lieutenant Commander Benjamin Finney, an instructor at Starfleet Academy.
A hypergiant observed one hundred years previously is expected to go supernova.
A new variant of the Starfleet uniform is introduced.
Doctor Leonard McCoy develops a medical procedure involving brain tissue engrafting.
Spock is assigned to the USS Enterprise as science officer.
Commander Finney is reprimanded for the incident aboard the Republic.
Commander Hansen Al-Salah begins requesting new supplies for the Earth Outpost Stations along the Romulan Neutral Zone.
Ensign James T. Kirk is assigned with Lieutenant Commander Benjamin Finney to the USS Republic.
Leonard McCoy becomes romantically involved with the future Nancy Crater.
Spock begins programming the.
Spock is promoted to Lieutenant.
The Enterprise, en route to Vega colony to hospitalize the Rigel VII victims, is diverted by an eighteen-year old distress call from the SS Columbia, crashed on Talos IV.

The Enterprise under the command of Christopher Pike, visits Rigel VII. While commanding the landing party, Pike enters an abandoned fortress and fights a Kalar warrior. The landing party's ambush leads to seven injuries and three deaths, including Pike's yeoman Zac Nguyen.
The Terran Empire rebellion establishes a base on Harlak.

While aboard the training vessel Republic during a change of watches, Ensign Kirk relieves Commander Finney and notes that a circuit, which should have been closed, to the atomic matter piles is opened. After closing the circuit, Kirk logs the incident.
Captain Matthew Decker finishes his second five-year mission aboard the Constellation.
Gabriel Lorca and Michael Burnham begin their attempted coup d'état against Emperor Philippa Georgiou.

James T. Kirk gets in a bar fight with Hendorff and three other cadets at the Shipyard Bar in Riverside. The fight is broken off by Captain Christopher Pike, who later talks to Kirk about joining Starfleet by reciting the incident resulting in the destruction of the USS Kelvin and his father, daring him to do better. The next day, Kirk boards the military shuttle Bardeen, enlisting in Starfleet.
James T. Kirk leaves Starfleet Academy. He is assigned to the phaser station aboard the USS Farragut.
Leonard McCoy gets divorced from his wife and enlists in Starfleet, boarding the same transport as Kirk, resulting in a friendship between the two.
On his first planetary survey mission, Lieutenant Kirk visits the planet Neural, where he meets Tyree.
Several Starfleet Academy cadets, including Nyota Uhura and Hendorff, spend some time in Riverside.
The Sheliak attempt to communicate with the Federation, something they will not do again until 2366.
The Terran Empire's new flagship, the ISS Charon, is launched from the Imperial Shipyard at Epsilon Indi IV.
The USS Enterprise undergoes construction at the Riverside Shipyard in Iowa.

A failed test of the USS Glenn’s experimental spore drive using a captured Tardigrade causes the crew to be mutilated. A group of Klingons try to take advantage of the disabled ship but are all killed by the tardigrade on board.

After being diverted from transfer to the Tellun penal colony, Michael Burnham and three other prisoners stay aboard the USS Discovery until their shuttle is fixed. While on board, Burnham learns about what the ship and her captain, Gabriel Lorca, are doing in their fight against the Klingons. Burnham is sent on an away mission with Sylvia Tilly and Paul Stamets to investigate the USS Glenn.
After finding that using the spore drive causes "Ripper" extreme pain, Michael Burnham releases the tardigrade from the Discovery. Its role is taken by Lieutenant Stamets.

Ambassador Sarek departs on a diplomatic mission, a meeting with a supposed dissenting Klingon faction. however his ship is bombed by a Logic extremist, stranding him inside a nebula until he can be retrieved in an unauthorized rescue mission by Michael Burnham. Katrina Cornwell goes in Sarek's place to meet with the Klingons, but is captured by them when the overture turns out to be a trap.
Captain Lorca has the Glenn scuttled, but recovers the tardigrade "Ripper" still on board and uses it to dramatically improve the Discovery’s spore drive.

Captain Lorca is captured by Klingons while returning from a conference. During his captivity, he encounters Starfleet Lieutenant Ash Tyler and Harcourt Fenton Mudd. Lorca and Tyler escape, leaving Mudd behind for helping the Klingons and are beamed aboard the Discovery after it arrives to find Lorca.

Harry Mudd uses a time crystal to trap Discovery inside a time loop in a attempt to figure out the starship's secret and sell the ship to the Klingons. Paul Stamets is able to exist outside of the loop and figures out his plan. In the end, Mudd is handed over to his fiance Stella and her father.

episodes.title: Magic to Make the Sanest Man Go Mad

events.summary: Leonard McCoy ends his relationship with (the future) Nancy Crater.
Michael Burnham joins the crew of the Discovery.
Starfleet begins conceptualizing the spore drive, installing it on two prototype vessels, the USS Glenn and the USS Discovery.
The Enterprise is ordered to sit out the Klingon War.
The Klingon reactionary leader T'Kuvma begins assembling like-minded Klingons into a nationalist, anti-Federation movement.

The USS Gagarin is ambushed simultaneously while patrolling its sector along with the two other Starfleet vessels in the area; the USS Hoover and USS Muroc. The Hoover and Muroc are destroyed, closely followed by the Gagarin after its warp drive is disabled. The USS Discovery responds to the Gagarin’s distress call but is unable to stop the Klingons destroying the vessel with all hands.
Voq is overthrown by Kol of the House of Kor, who assumes leadership of T'Kuvma's Klingons. Voq is abandoned aboard the wreck of the USS Shenzhou but is rescued by L'Rell.

episodes.title: The Butcher's Knife Cares Not for the Lamb's Cry

events.summary: With the aid of the spore drive, Captain Lorca leads the Discovery to victory, destroying multiple Klingon ships during their attack on Corvan II.

episodes.title: The Butcher's Knife Cares Not for the Lamb's Cry

events.summary: Zefram Cochrane Warp Research Facility opens.

August: Montgomery Scott and Keenser are stationed at the Federation outpost at Delta Vega in the Vulcan system six months before their encounter with James T. Kirk and Spock on stardate 2258.42. Scott believed the assignment was a punishment for losing Admiral Archer's prized pet beagle in a transwarp beaming experiment.
Christopher Pike assumes command of the Discovery.

Commander Saru leads the Discovery on a mission that results in the end of the Federation-Klingon War, with many of the crew members being awarded for their actions, including Saru receiving the Starfleet Medal of Honor.
Following a number of multiple catastrophic systems failures, The Enterprise is towed to spacedock for repairs.
Lieutenant Commander Una Chin-Riley begins an investigation into the allegations against Spock.

Spock takes a leave of absence from Starfleet and commits himself to the psychiatric ward on Starbase 5. During his time there, he supposedly murders two nurses and steals a shuttlecraft to follow the path of the red bursts.

The Discovery crew, still led by Saru, make their way to Vulcan to pick up the ship's new commanding officer, but stop along the way to respond to a distress call from Captain Christopher Pike aboard the USS Enterprise.
The Discovery makes first contact with the New Eden colony on the planet Terralysium.
The Shakespearian troupe Karidian Company of Players, sponsored by the Galactic Cultural Exchange Project, begins its tour of official installations.
The USS Discovery returns from the mirror universe nine months after they left the prime universe.

episodes.title: The War Without, The War Within

events.summary: Two hundred crewmembers of the Farragut are killed at Tycho IV by a dikironium cloud creature. One of the casualties is the ship's commanding officer, Captain Garrovick. The Farragut first officer commends Lieutenant Kirk for his actions at Tycho IV.
Admiral Alexander Marcus discovers the SS Botany Bay and recovers Khan Noonien Singh, forcibly recruiting him into Starfleet under the name of John Harrison.
Ash Tyler is offered command of Section 31.

Christopher Pike is promoted to admiral. James T. Kirk becomes captain of the USS Enterprise as Christopher Pike's relief. Ambassador Spock leads survivors of Vulcan in forming a New Vulcan colony with advice to his younger self to continue in Starfleet.
Christopher Pike returns to the Enterprise to resume command.
Citing Starfleet Regulation 157, section 3, Lt. Spock recommends to Starfleet that USS Discovery, its personnel, and its spore drive should not be discussed under penalty of treason.
Doctor Joseph M'Benga's daughter Rukiya is diagnosed with cygnokemia.

En route, cadet James Kirk, who has stowed away with the help of Medical Officer Leonard "Bones" McCoy, overhears the mission briefing and deduces that the circumstances around Vulcan are similar to those surrounding his birth, when the USS Kelvin was destroyed by an unidentified, technologically-advanced ship. He reports these conclusions to Pike, who thusly prepares the Enterprise for action.
In the official records of Starfleet, the USS Discovery is listed as destroyed. The records do not mention the spore drive, Red Angel, or Control.

James T. Kirk takes the Kobayashi Maru test for the third time, but this time he manages to secretly install a subroutine which changes the conditions of the test, thereby allowing Kirk to save the Kobayashi Maru and beat the test, becoming the only cadet to ever beat the no-win scenario.
Katie Wesley is born to Bob Wesley.

episodes.title: One of Our Planets Is Missing

events.summary: Kirk and Spock board the Narada to confront Nero and stop him. Spock steals the Jellyfish and Kirk fights with several guards, Ayel and eventually Nero. The Jellyfish destroys the drilling platform and escapes the area, to draw the battle away from Earth and sets a collision course for the Narada. He and Kirk along with Pike are transported away at the last second by the Enterprise, but the Jellyfish crashes into the Narada thereby igniting the red matter and causing a massive black hole, which consumes the Narada, and very nearly the Enterprise as well. This beam-out marks the first time Scott ever beams three people from two targets onto one pad.

Kirk, Olson and Sulu space-dive onto the platform. Olson, who had the charges, is killed in the descent; however, Kirk and Sulu succeed at disabling the drill. Unfortunately, the drill's work has already been completed, and Nero orders the red matter deployed. Kirk and Sulu beam back to the ship by the efforts of Ensign Pavel Chekov.

Nero is first seen by the Federation and tells the native Spock that he wants him to see something as well as order Captain Pike to come aboard the Narada, just like he requested Richard Robau to twenty-five years earlier.

On stardate 2258.42 (February 11), the planet Vulcan is destroyed along with, approximately, six billion Vulcans on-planet by Nero using red matter. The Vulcan species is rendered critically endangered with only an estimated ten thousand surviving. Captain Pike is taken hostage by aboard the Narada by Nero.
Pike agrees and is accompanied by Kirk, helmsman Hikaru Sulu, and chief engineer Olson. Pike appoints Spock acting captain and Kirk his first officer.

Spock and Kirk meet Montgomery Scott and Keenser at the Delta Vega outpost, and using his transwarp beaming equation, Spock transports Kirk and Scott to the Enterprise. After a fight which proves Spock to be emotionally compromised, Kirk assumes command and the pursuit of Nero. Meanwhile, Nero and the Narada head for Earth intent on destroying the planet. Chekov devises a plan to hide the Enterprise near Saturn to avoid the Narada’s detection and rescue Captain Pike.

Spock determines that the red matter will cause a black hole to develop inside Vulcan. While the planet evacuates, he beams down to reach several Vulcan dignitaries who reside in areas that are unreachable by transporter. He succeeds at saving some of them, including his father Sarek, but his mother Amanda Grayson is killed.

Spock, emotionally unbalanced by these traumas, orders a course for the Laurentian system to rendezvous with the rest of Starfleet in an effort to balance the odds for the next confrontation against Nero. Kirk objects and is marooned on Delta Vega. He gets chased by vicious animals and finds his rescuer is Spock himself, but 129 years older. Spock explains the situation to Kirk via a mind meld and they head for the Starfleet outpost fourteen kilometers away.

The Enterprise arrives only to find a debris field of the fleet, and the Narada hovering over Vulcan with a drilling platform in the lower atmosphere attempting to mine to the core. Nero orders the destruction of the Enterprise, only to realize what vessel he is firing on.

The Narada, a Romulan mining vessel from the future of the prime reality, destroys 47 Klingon warbirds near the Klingon prison planet. Later, it arrives at a location where the Jellyfish carrying Spock emerges from a black hole, created in the future to stop a star that had gone supernova and threatened the galaxy. Nero, captain of the Narada, captures the Jellyfish and maroons Spock on Delta Vega so he can observe the destruction of Vulcan.
The newly-repaired USS Enterprise departs from spacedock on a mission to Edrin II.
The USS Discovery travels to 3189 via a wormhole.

Under the command of James T. Kirk, the Enterprise begins its mission with the crew who helped him stop Nero, including Leonard McCoy, Nyota Uhura, Hikaru Sulu, Pavel Chekov, Montgomery Scott, and Spock.
Vulcan itself is destroyed. Spock privately estimates no more than 10,000 survivors, making him a member of an endangered species.
A new First Servant is selected on Majalis.

episodes.title: Lift Us Where Suffering Cannot Reach

events.summary: A new variant of Starfleet uniform is introduced.
A new variant of the Starfleet communicator is introduced.
A new variant of the Starfleet flag officer uniform is introduced.
A new variant of the Starfleet tricorder is introduced.
A new variant of the Type 2 phaser is introduced.
After crash landing on Valeo Beta V, the crew is killed by Gorn hatchlings. The Peregrine is later salvaged by the Enterprise.
As of this year, the Ketha Province has been uninhabited for decades.

At Starfleet Headquarters, Marcus convenes a summit to commence a manhunt for Harrison. Harrison attacks the summit, murdering Pike. Harrison escapes by transwarp beaming to the uninhabited Ketha Province of Qo'noS. Kirk receives permission to hunt down Harrison and to reinstate Spock as his first officer. Admiral Marcus orders Kirk to use seventy-two experimental long-range photon torpedoes to execute Harrison from orbit.

Both damaged ships begin falling to Earth. Kirk enters the radiation-flooded warp core, and is able to repair it, narrowly saving the Enterprise, but at the cost of his life. Khan deliberately pilots the Vengeance towards San Francisco, crashing it in the city center. Khan survives, and flees through San Francisco. However, he is apprehended by Spock and Uhura. Due to Khan's regenerative blood cells, a blood transfusion is conducted from Khan to Kirk.
Cadet Uhura is promoted to Ensign.
Commander Chin-Riley is cleared of all charges against her and resumes her duties aboard the Enterprise.
Commander Pelia joins the Enterprise as chief engineer.
General Order 1 is renamed the "Prime Directive".
In an alternate timeline, Captain James T. Kirk is the commanding officer of the Enterprise.
James T. Kirk is promoted to First officer.
January: The crew of the USS Enterprise become involved in the Mudd Incident, leading them to confiscate a K'normian trading ship.
John Harrison coerces Thomas Harewood a member of Section 31 into bombing their facility under the Kelvin Memorial Archive, in London.
Rukiya is cured of her illness and is transformed into a state of pure energy.
Spock and T'Pring get engaged.

Stardate 1224.3. The Enterprise travels to Hetemit IX to investigate a former Illyrian colony. During the mission, Captain Christopher Pike and Lieutenant Spock are trapped on the planet while the landing party are exposed to a light-addicting virus.
Stardate 1457.9. Lieutenant Commander Chin-Riley is taken into custody for violating the Starfleet code of Conduct.
Stardate 1630.1. The Enterprise returns to Rigel VII under suspicion of cultural contamination, discovering and arresting former yeoman Zac Nguyen in the process.
Stardate 2394.8. Captain Pike is promoted to Fleet captain.
Stardate 2394.8. Fleet captain Pike is temporarily given command of the Farragut.
Stardate 2912.4. The Enterprise makes first contact with the Shepherds.

Stardate 3177.3-3177.9. The first known contact between vessels representing the United Federation of Planets and the Gorn Hegemony occurs after Enterprise encounters several Gorn ships after they attack the Finibus III colony. Actual contact between crews is not made.
The Archer is tasked with making first contact with the Kiley.
The Farragut is stationed at Starbase 1.
The planet Cerberus is stricken by crop failure during Joanna McCoy's schooling there. Carter Winston uses his personal fortune to save the colony.
The R'ongovian Protectorate joins the United Federation of Planets.
The Stamets-type shuttlecraft is introduced.
This is the year James T. Kirk was expected to graduate from Starfleet Academy after entering in 2255, assuming he took four years to do so and not three as he declared he would, and ultimately did.
Two weeks later, Kirk is fully revived. Khan is placed back in his old cryotube once more.
Una Chin-Riley is given command of the USS Archer.
Captain Matthew Decker finishes his third five-year mission aboard the Constellation.
Carol Marcus officially joins the Enterprise crew.
Following extensive repairs, the USS Enterprise embarks on the first five-year mission of uncharted deep space exploration.
Nearly a year after stardate 2259.55: A fully recovered James T. Kirk gives a stirring speech about the principles of the Federation at a ceremony to honor those lost nearly a year prior.
The Cayuga is destroyed by the Gorn over Parnassus Beta.
The Enterprise’s first day in deep space.
The Stardiver is destroyed by the Gorn in the Shangdi system.
Cyrano Jones, a licensed asteroid locator and prospector, begins selling and buying rare merchandise.
Dr. Roger Korby sends his last transmission from the planet of Exo III.

episodes.title: What Are Little Girls Made Of?

events.summary: James T. Kirk is romantically involved with Janet Wallace.
La´an Noonien-Singh is tasked with testing Starfleet's new technology recreation room, or holodeck to see if it can withstand genuine rigorous circumstances.
On 892-IV, Flavius Maximus wins his first arena game. For the next seven years, he was the most successful gladiator of his province.
Professor Robert Crater and his wife Nancy arrive on the planet M-113. The professor begins his archaeological excavation of an ancient and extinct civilization that once lived there.
Spock meets Leila Kalomi on Earth.
The United Federation of Planets celebrates 100 years since its founding
The USS Enterprise goes on an undercover mission the pre-warp planet Tezaar. Four of the five members of the away team have their dna temporarily altered to appear Vulcan to the sensors on Tezaar.
Areel Shaw and James T. Kirk meet each other for the last time before Kirk's trial on Starbase 11 in 2267.
R.M. Merik becomes the Lord of the Games on 892-IV.
SS Beagle crash-lands on 892-IV after having sustained heavy meteor damage. The ship's crew is captured by the inhabitants.
Stardate 2262.18 (January 18): James T. Kirk logs a entry in his captain's log. The issues Behemoth, Part 1 and Behemoth, Part 2 of the Star Trek: Ongoing take place around this date.
Stardate 2262.248 (September 5): James T. Kirk logs a entry in his captain's log.
A peace offering to the Teenaxi Delegation from the Fibonan Republic with Captain James T. Kirk acting on their behalf goes awry forcing Kirk to abort the negotiation.
Ambassador Spock dies on New Vulcan.
An infant Lactran, later encountered by the USS Enterprise landing party in 2269, is born on Lactra VII.
Ben Childress, Herm Gossett, and Benton partner up to establish a mining company on Rigel XII.
Construction for the USS Enterprise is underway at Starbase Yorktown.
James T. Kirk celebrates his 30th birthday at a surprise party, during which he honors the Enterprise and those who did not survive recent events.
Jaylah is offered aceeptance into Starfleet Academy after Kirk "pulled a few strings".
Krall launches an assault on Starbase Yorktown, but is ultimately defeated by the remaining Enterprise crew in the repaired Franklin.
Matthew Decker is promoted to commodore. He remains in command of the USS Constellation.
Montgomery Scott is led to the long-lost and intact USS Franklin on Altamid by Jaylah.

One hundred fifty men, women, and children, led by Elias Sandoval, depart Earth for Omicron Ceti III to establish the future Omicron colony. It is the last time they will encounter anyone outside their own group until the USS Enterprise's 2267 arrival.
Spock goes through a cache of items belonging to the late Ambassador Spock and discovers a photo of him with the senior crew of the USS Enterprise circa 2287 in his timeline.
The Enterprise is destroyed by Krall and his swarm ships during an alleged rescue mission over Altamid.
The USS Enterprise makes a reprovisioning stop at Yorktown Station.
Carter Winston disappears in a meteor storm. His spacecraft crashes on the planet Vendor, where he is saved by an inhabitant of the planet.
In an alternate timeline, Captain James T. Kirk is the commanding officer of the USS Enterprise.
In the same timeline, Commander Thelin becomes the first officer of the Enterprise.
Spock visits his parents on Vulcan for the last time in the next four years.
The Omicron colony is established at Omicron Ceti III.
The USS Reliant is launched.
Tuvok is born on Vulcanis Lunar colony.
A bottle from this year's vintage of Dom Pérignon will be used in the christening of the USS Enterprise in 2293.
A new uniform is introduced for Starfleet personnel, though the previous style continues to be used for years. These new uniforms remain in service until the early 2270s.
April Wade is born.

At a Christmas party held in the Enterprise science lab, Captain Kirk meets Dr. Helen Noel. She later recalls him "dropping in" on the party, during a mission to the rehabilitation colony on Tantalus V.
Captain Christopher Pike is again promoted to the rank of fleet captain.

Captain James T. Kirk assumes command of the USS Enterprise for an historic five-year mission of exploration. He is seen off on his first mission as commander of the starship by his brother Sam and his family. His father is also proud to see him off. Before assuming command, Kirk's emotional structure is tested by the Robbiani dermal-optic test.

episodes.title: What Are Little Girls Made Of?

events.summary: Carter Winston dies on the planet Vendor.
Ingraham B is attacked by flying parasites.
Nancy Crater is killed by planet M-113's last surviving native.

The Borg "swarm through" the El-Aurian system, invading and destroying the El-Aurian homeworld. The survivors flee and are scattered over the entire galaxy, with some reaching Earth twenty-eight years later.

The Enterprise becomes one of the first Earth ships to reach the edge of the Milky Way Galaxy, where they encounter the galactic barrier. Gary Mitchell and Elizabeth Dehner are among the Enterprise officers who die in the line of duty.

episodes.title: Where No Man Has Gone Before

events.summary: The Klingons and the Federation sign a peace treaty which they honor over the next three years.
A Romulan ship crosses into Federation space and destroys several Earth Outpost Stations, ending over a century of Romulan isolation. This incident comes to be known as the Neutral Zone Incursion.
A transport from Ingraham B to Deneva carries the deadly flying parasite that caused mass insanity on Ingraham B in 2265.
Dr. Leonard McCoy replaces Dr. Mark Piper as ship's surgeon and chief medical officer aboard the USS Enterprise, some time after 2265.

episodes.title: Where No Man Has Gone Before
Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country

events.summary: Dr. McCoy spills acid in sickbay, an event which also apparently happens on the mirror universe ISS Enterprise at some point prior to 2267.
Dr. Simon Van Gelder is assigned to the Tantalus Penal Colony as associate director, serving on Tristan Adams' staff.
Due to a transporter malfunction, Captain James T. Kirk is temporarily split into a positive and a negative half.

Famed archeologist Roger Korby is found alive on the planet Exo III, where he discovered remains of ancient technology. However, it turns out Korby is actually an android duplicate of his former self. He perishes along with the other androids on the surface.

episodes.title: What Are Little Girls Made Of?

events.summary: Kodos the Executioner is discovered to still be alive, despite having been thought dead since 2246. While under surveillance by Kirk, Kodos is accidentally killed by his daughter Lenore before formal charges can be brought against him for crimes committed on Tarsus IV.
Lieutenant Hikaru Sulu transfers from staff physicist to helm officer aboard the USS Enterprise.

Lieutenant Nyota Uhura becomes chief communications officer and Janice Rand becomes the captain's yeoman on the USS Enterprise. Dave Bailey is promoted to lieutenant and chief navigator despite some concerns about his lack of experience.
On board the USS Enterprise, Dr. McCoy, in his quarters, kills the last M-113 creature as it tries to kill Kirk. The M-113 creature had already killed four Enterprise crewmen.

Six months before the Neutral Zone Incursion, an accident aboard a class J starship releases delta radiation, killing several Starfleet cadets and severely injuring Fleet Captain Christopher Pike, confining him to a wheelchair.
The Antares is destroyed, with all hands on board perishing, when Charles Evans with psionic powers makes a baffle plate on the shield of the ship's energy pile "go away".
The colonists of Janus VI create a detailed series of charts for the twenty-two levels of the mine. These charts later prove useful in the search for the Horta in 2267.
The inhabitants of Acamar III establish peace after generations of wars. An exception are the Gatherers, who continue to be violent for a century.
The planet Psi 2000 disintegrates naturally.
The Redjac entity, incarnated as "Beratis", kills several women on Rigel IV.
The UFP makes first contact with the First Federation after an encounter with Balok, commander of the flagship Fesarius. As an intercultural exchange, Dave Bailey transfers to serve on the vessel.
The USS Enterprise charts the L-370 system.
The USS Enterprise undergoes a refit both on the outside and the inside design sometime after 2265.

episodes.title: Where No Man Has Gone Before

events.summary: A transporter accident brings four USS Enterprise personnel from the primary universe to the mirror universe. The primary Kirk encourages the mirror Spock to start a revolution against the Terran Empire. This eventually led to the conquest of that Empire by the Klingon-Cardassian Alliance in the late 23rd century.
Although not generally revealed, the USS Enterprise discovers warp drive inventor Zefram Cochrane, long believed dead, alive on a remote planetoid in the Gamma Canaris region.
Dr. Janice Lester leads an expedition of scientists to Camus II to investigate the civilization that once existed on that planet.
Ensign Pavel Chekov joins the crew of the USS Enterprise. Before the end of the year, Chekov becomes chief navigator.

episodes.title: Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan

events.summary: Except for Captain Ronald Tracey, the entire crew of the USS Exeter is killed while in orbit around the planet Omega IV by an unknown disease, which turns them into dehydrated crystals composed of their base minerals.
Fleet Captain Christopher Pike is taken by Commander Spock and the USS Enterprise to the planet Talos IV, goes into retirement, and lives with Vina in illusory bliss.
Flying parasites invade the Deneva colony. Kirk and the crew of the USS Enterprise discover their weakness and destroy them, ending the spread of mass insanity.

Helmsman Hikaru Sulu's command console explodes in his face aboard the USS Enterprise and Doctor Leonard McCoy accidentally gives himself an overdose of cordrazine which, although not generally revealed, nearly leads him to change history erasing the Federation and Starfleet from existence.

episodes.title: The City on the Edge of Forever

events.summary: Nimbus III is jointly established as the "Planet of Galactic Peace" by the Romulan Star Empire, the United Federation of Planets, and the Klingon Empire.

episodes.title: Star Trek V: The Final Frontier

events.summary: Starfleet loses contact with John Gill, a Federation cultural observer, on Ekos. They continue their attempts to contact him before sending the Enterprise there to investigate.

The Enterprise visits a mysterious planet in the Omicron Delta region for some much needed shore leave. However, the planet is discovered to be more than the isolated world it appears to be. The planet contains multiple fantasy and wish-fulfillment elements found in the crew's minds. Dr. Leonard McCoy is believed to be killed by an illusory Black Knight. After Captain Kirk and Commander Spock come into contact with the Keeper, McCoy is revived and the Enterprise crew is ultimately able to rest and relax before departing.

The Federation observation outpost on the planet Cestus III is destroyed which leads the USS Enterprise to make first contact with the Gorn, who are responsible for the outpost's destruction as well as the deaths of two Enterprise crewmen, because the Gorn claim the outpost was in their territory. The Enterprise also makes first contact with the Metrons, who become impressed with Humanity when Captain Kirk shows mercy and spares the life of a Gorn captain after the Metrons set up an arena-style conflict between the two of them.
The Klingons provide the inhabitants of Neural with firearms, trying to influence local politics.
The original USS Enterprise shuttlecraft Galileo is incinerated and destroyed. Fortunately, the five crew members on board are saved before its destruction via the transporter.

The population of the Federation experimental colony on planet Gamma Hydra IV is killed from rapid aging caused by radiation from a rogue comet. The USS Enterprise landing party, sent to investigate, also suffers from rapid aging, except for Ensign Chekov, including the ship's four senior officers which results in the death of a science division lieutenant. Fortunately, Kirk, Spock, Dr. McCoy, and Lieutenant Commander Montgomery Scott are cured of the rapid aging when McCoy, with the help of Spock, Nurse Christine Chapel, and Dr. Janet Wallace, discovers an adrenaline-based cure.
The SS Botany Bay is discovered by the USS Enterprise in the Mutara sector after drifting in space for two hundred and seventy-one years.
The Treaty of Organia is signed by the Klingon Empire and the United Federation of Planets, thereby preventing a destructive conflict between the two powers and paving the way for further approaches.
The USS Constellation is destroyed by eliminating an alien planet killer. Commodore Matt Decker and his entire crew are killed by the planet killer.

The USS Enterprise uncovers the truth behind the disappearance of the starship Archon that had been lost a hundred years earlier, when it visited the planet Beta III. The Enterprise is responsible for the destruction of the computer known as Landru which had ruled Beta III as a despot for six thousand years. The ship leaves behind its sociologist Lindstrom and a team of specialists to restore that planet's culture to a more Human form.
A brief alliance between the Klingon Empire and the Romulan Star Empire occurs. This results in an exchange of technology and spacecraft design, most notably the cloaking device to the Klingons.

A Federation vessel makes first contact with the Children of Tama, but formal relations cannot be established due an inability to understand the Tamarian language, which is described as incomprehensible.
A Slaver stasis box is discovered by archaeologists on the planet Kzin.

All the adults of the Starnes Exploration Party are killed, due to the alien Gorgan's influence on the children, on the Federation outpost planet Triacus. Gorgan is destroyed, a short time later, aboard the USS Enterprise.
Ceti Alpha VI explodes and turns the former M class planet Ceti Alpha V into a barren wasteland.

episodes.title: Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan

events.summary: Dr. McCoy contracts the rare illness xenopolycythemia but is cured a short time later via the Fabrini's vast database.

episodes.title: For the World is Hollow and I Have Touched the Sky

events.summary: Dr. McCoy, with the help of Lieutenant Brent and an operations division lieutenant, administers to the inmates/patients of Elba II asylum a revolutionary new medicine that potentially could eliminate mental illness.
Dr. Miranda Jones boards a Medusan vessel to help ready Medusans to be navigators on Federation starships.

episodes.title: Is There in Truth No Beauty?

events.summary: Dr. Richard Daystrom suffers a mental breakdown aboard the USS Enterprise.

Federation cultural observer and former history teacher of Captain Kirk, John Gill, is shot and killed on the planet Ekos by Melakon after Gill denounces his own idea of a Nazi regime set up on Ekos as well as their battle against the Zeon and the subjugation and extermination of Zeon's population on a star system wide television broadcast. Melakon is in turn shot and killed by Isak. Thereafter, Isak and Ekosians Daras and Eneg agree to end the violence and set Ekos on a more humane path.
Harry Mudd steals a spaceship to escape the androids from the Andromeda Galaxy.
James T. Kirk oversees a landing party on Argus X in order to search for the dikironium cloud creature. This expedition is assisted by Ensign Garrovick and security officers Bardoli and Swenson.
Kirk and Spock reprogram the Yonada’s course to the one the Fabrini originally intended.

episodes.title: For the World is Hollow and I Have Touched the Sky

events.summary: Kirk, Commander Spock, and Doctor Leonard McCoy are able to save the mute native humanoid species of Gamma Vertis IV from the destruction of the star Minara going supernova by showing the Vian scientists Thann and Lal that Gem and her mute species can express love and compassion.
Kirk invents Fizzbin.

Lieutenant Commander and Dr. Ann Mulhall joins the crew of the USS Enterprise. An astrobiologist, Ann Mulhall is the highest ranking female officer to serve aboard the Enterprise during its original five-year mission.
Lokai steals a shuttlecraft from Starbase 4.

episodes.title: Let That Be Your Last Battlefield

events.summary: McCoy is briefly married to Natira.

episodes.title: For the World is Hollow and I Have Touched the Sky

events.summary: Odona becomes patient zero (although she is cured of the disease herself) and introduces the disease Vegan choriomeningitis to her people on Gideon as a way to control the population on that planet.
One of the designers of the USS Enterprise and Federation engineer Dr. Larry Marvick dies a short time after looking at Medusan Ambassador Kollos.

episodes.title: Is There in Truth No Beauty?

events.summary: The Defiant is discovered by the USS Enterprise adrift in the interphase of Tholian space with all of the crew dead due to mass insanity. After Kirk is rescued from the ship, unknown to the Federation and Starfleet, the USS Defiant, courtesy of a Tholian tri-cobalt warhead, ends up in the mirror universe in 2155 stolen by Commander Jonathan Archer and the crew of the ISS Enterprise.

The Dohlman of Elas named Elaan is married, in an arranged union, to the ruler of Troyius in the hopes of fostering peace between the planet Elas and the planet Troyius, which are both in the Tellun star system.
The Enterprise makes first contact with androids from the Andromeda Galaxy. This is made difficult at first due to the interference of Harcourt Fenton Mudd.
The Keeper of the Shore Leave Planet dies.
The planet Amerind is saved from destruction by an asteroid by Kirk and Spock.

The planet Cheron's humanoid population destroys their cities and kills each other off in a civil war, making much of the planet uninhabitable to humanoid lifeforms; lower species animals encroach on and take over the cities.

episodes.title: Let That Be Your Last Battlefield

events.summary: The Polaric Test Ban Treaty is signed after the near destruction of a Romulan research colony due to a polaric ion device explosion on Chaltok IV.
The star Minara goes supernova.
The United Federation of Planets hosts the Babel Conference, which eventually results in Coridan's admission to the Federation.

The USS Enterprise makes first contact with intergalactic beings the Kelvans from the Andromeda Galaxy. This first contact is at first difficult as the Kelvans attempt to hijack the Enterprise and kill one of the crew.

The USS Enterprise makes first contact with the Eymorgs of Sigma Draconis VI after Kara steals Spock's brain to be used as the new Controller for that planet. McCoy puts Spock's brain back in Spock's head with the help of the Great Teacher, with the Eymorgs being forced to live, again, on the surface of that planet with the Morgs.
The USS Enterprise makes first contact with the xenophobic Melkotians.
The USS Enterprise visits Deep Space Station K-7.
The USS Intrepid is destroyed and its entire crew of Vulcans killed, as well as the destruction of the Gamma 7A system, by an enormous space going single-celled organism.
Captain James T. Kirk, Commander Spock, and historian Erickson use the Guardian of Forever to observe Federation and Orion history firsthand.

Dr. Leonard McCoy discovers that Flint, found by the USS Enterprise living on the planet Holberg 917G, has his time as an immortal coming to an end and will soon die, due to his leaving Earth's atmosphere. Although not revealed during his lifetime, as an immortal Flint was known by several historic names including King Solomon, Alexander the Great, Leonardo da Vinci, Johannes Brahms, and interstellar financier Brack.
Dr. Sevrin and his follower Adam die on Eden after biting into an acid-saturated fruit.
Dr. Sevrin and his followers temporarily hijack the USS Enterprise, steal the shuttlecraft Galileo II, and take both into Romulan space so they can get to the planet Eden.
Els Renora is born on Bajor.
Harry Mudd swindles the inhabitants of the planet Ilyra VI by trying to sell them the Starfleet Academy.
Keiko Ishikawa's mother is born.
Lieutenant Arex replaces Ensign Pavel Chekov as the USS Enterprise’s chief navigator as Chekov was assigned further officer training.
Lieutenant M'Ress is assigned to be a relief communications officer for Lieutenant Uhura aboard the USS Enterprise.
Lieutenant Mira Romaine begins working to restore the memory core of Memory Alpha.

Memory Alpha's memory core is burned out, and all of its personnel, Federation scholars, researchers, and scientists that are there at the time are killed, when the planetoid is attacked by the "lights of Zetar" beings.
The asteroid ship Yonada reaches its destination and the Fabrini settle down under the leadership of high priestess Natira.

episodes.title: For the World is Hollow and I Have Touched the Sky

events.summary: The Enterprise makes first contact with the Excalbians.
The Enterprise re-establishes contact with the Megans of the planet Megas-Tu as the ship is exploring the galactic core.
The Federation class J space cruiser Aurora is stolen by Doctor Sevrin and his followers.
The Mantilles colony in the Pallas 14 system is threatened by the cosmic cloud creature.

episodes.title: One of Our Planets Is Missing

events.summary: The star Beta Niobe supernovas, destroying the planet Sarpeidon.
The Troglyte miners of the planet Ardana start wearing filter masks to block the mental ill effects of mining zenite.
The USS Enterprise discovers an ancient abandoned insectoid starship in orbit around Questar M-17 and makes first contact with a magnetic organism.
The USS Enterprise uncovers what happened with the Human geneticist Dr. Stavos Keniclius by finding his fifth generation clone living with the Phylosians.

The USS Enterprise uncovers what happened with the Terra 10 colonists, with whom Earth lost contact in the middle of the 21st century, and help their mutated Terratin descendants relocate to a geologically more stable planet than the one they are found on orbiting the star Cepheus.
Tuvok begins taking lessons in kal-toh from a Vulcan master.

After a mild outbreak of choriocytosis aboard the USS Enterprise, the only seriously ill crewmember, Commander Spock, is saved from near death by a shipment of strobolin which was briefly stolen by Orions.

After the testimony of Kol-Tai, the defense witness for McCoy, it is discovered that the auroral plague was caused by radiation; McCoy is exonerated and lauded as a hero for saving both the lives on Dramia II and his own crewmates.
Captain James T. Kirk invokes Starfleet General Order 6 on the USS Enterprise after he and his crew contract the auroral plague.
Felisa Howard is born.
Kirk is promoted to admiral and becomes Chief of Starfleet Operations.

Kirk orders a Federation quarantine of the planet Delta Theta III under Starfleet General Order 1 to maintain the Prime Directive of non-interference in the development of that planet's society and culture.
Leonard McCoy also retires from Starfleet and starts a private medical practice on Earth.
McCoy finds a cure for the auroral plague, via blood samples of the USS Enterprise crew, which allows Kirk to lift General Order 6.
Near the beginning of this year, James T. Kirk's five-year mission ends.
On the planet Dramia, Commander Demos place Doctor Leonard McCoy under arrest for allowing the outbreak of an auroral plague to develop on Dramia II in 2251.
Orion neutrality comes to an end after an act of piracy. After the freighter USS Huron is attacked and looted, the USS Enterprise captures the Orion vessel responsible.
Spock retires from Starfleet and returns to Vulcan to undergo Kolinahr.
Will Decker is promoted to captain of the USS Enterprise.

During an encounter with Romulans near the Romulan Neutral Zone in 2267, James T. Kirk claimed that the USS Enterprise would self destruct, and that the area of space it was inhabiting should be avoided by starships until this year due to the danger posed by its "Corbomite device."
Kor leads the Klingons in the Battle of Klach D'kel Brakt against the Romulans, marking the end of the Romulan-Klingon Alliance.
Two years prior to this year, Spock said that in two standard years, the Skorr could breed an army of up to two hundred billion warriors.
The Klingon Empire relinquishes its claim on the Archanis sector to the Federation.
Sarek of Vulcan meets the Legarans for the first time.
SS Artemis departs from Mars on a mission to colonize Septimis Minor.

A group of young Ba'ku wanting to follow the ways of the offlanders (people not born on the Ba'ku planet) attempt to take over the colony and fail. For their punishment, they are exiled and will find a new identity as the Son'a.
Tressa is born on Drayan II.
Chu'lak is born in the S'Lara region.
Pardek becomes a senator.
Three weeks out of her home starbase, the Federation starship USS Bozeman disappears near the Typhon Expanse.
Mark Jameson is born.
The border treaty between the Bajoran factions Paqu and Navot on Bajor is signed.
A female Prime was born on K'Tuevon Prime this year. One hundred years later, her father rented an event silo to celebrate her 100th birthday.
James T. Kirk sees Demora Sulu for the last time until 2293.
Chez DeSalle begins to develop their pod plant purée recipe.
In Idaho on Earth, James T. Kirk takes one of his uncle's horses for a ride and meets Antonia.
Audrid Dax dies. The Dax symbiont passes to Torias.
Carol Marcus sends a next phase proposal for Project Genesis to the Federation authorities.

episodes.title: Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan

events.summary: James T. Kirk tells Antonia his plans to return to Starfleet.
Rishon Uxbridge is born.
Zek takes his last vacation.

Admiral Kirk steals the USS Enterprise in an attempt to rescue Spock, revived by the effects of the Genesis Planet. USS Grissom is destroyed by a Klingon Bird-of-Prey commanded by Kruge. The Enterprise is scuttled to prevent it from falling into Klingon hands. Kirk and his crew return to Vulcan in the Klingon Bird-of-Prey, where Spock's memory is restored with the fal-tor-pan ritual.

episodes.title: Star Trek III: The Search for Spock

events.summary: Following the death of Torias in a shuttlecraft accident, the Dax symbiont is joined with Joran.
Initial performance tests of the Excelsior’s transwarp drive are a failure and the ship remains in Spacedock for the next two years.
Joran writes a musical piece.

Project Genesis enters its third stage. USS Reliant is dispatched to the Ceti Alpha star system to determine the viability of Ceti Alpha VI for use in the Genesis test. The vessel is hijacked by Khan Noonien Singh and used to launch an attack on the USS Enterprise. Khan is killed in the detonation of the Genesis Device aboard Reliant. Captain Spock dies while restoring power to Enterprise’s warp drive, allowing it to escape. The Genesis Planet is formed.

episodes.title: Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan

events.summary: (Stardate 8130.3): James T. Kirk turns 52 years old.

episodes.title: Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan

events.summary: The USS Excelsior, Starfleet's first Excelsior starship, is commissioned.

episodes.title: Star Trek III: The Search for Spock

events.summary: USS Hathaway, a Constellation starship, is launched from Copernicus Ship Yards, Luna.

After spending three months in exile on Vulcan, Kirk and his crew are put on trial for their actions during the Genesis incident. All charges but one are dismissed, and Kirk is demoted to the rank of captain in punishment for the remaining charge.

episodes.title: Star Trek IV: The Voyage Home

events.summary: An alien probe, dubbed the "Whale Probe", disables Starfleet and Klingon ships and causes serious damage to Earth's atmosphere. It is discovered that the probe was sent to contact the species Megaptera novaeangliae or humpback whale, which had, however, been hunted to extinction in the 21st century. Admiral James T. Kirk is able to retrieve two members of the species from the year 1986, and avert further catastrophe. The probe leaves Federation space, and the disabled ships are restored.

episodes.title: Star Trek IV: The Voyage Home

events.summary: Butler, Kirk's dog, dies.
Due to the Whale Probe threat, Earth enters a state of emergency, a state that is not utilized again until the Borg threat of 2366.
On Acamar III, the Tralesta clan is almost exterminated by their archenemies from the Lornak clan in the Tralesta Massacre.
Six months after joining with the Dax symbiont, Joran dies. The symbiont is then joined with Curzon.
The USS Enterprise is launched under the command of Captain Kirk.

episodes.title: Star Trek IV: The Voyage Home

events.summary: The Yorktown under the command of Captain Joel Randolph. While deployed near the Sol system, the Yorktown was one of the many vessels disabled by the Whale Probe near Earth.

episodes.title: Star Trek IV: The Voyage Home

events.summary: While patrolling Sector 5 of the Neutral Zone, the Saratoga encountered a probe of unknown origin on a direct course to Earth.

episodes.title: Star Trek IV: The Voyage Home

events.summary: After the journey to Sha Ka Ree, the senior staff of the Enterprise-A takes a photograph together on the bridge. Spock keeps this image for the rest of his life until his death in 2263 of the and it is passed on to his alternate self.

episodes.title: Star Trek V: The Final Frontier

events.summary: Captain James T. Kirk and the crew of the new USS Enterprise are pressed back into service. Sybok hijacks the Enterprise and forces the ship to travel through the Great Barrier in search of the planet Sha Ka Ree and God. They find Sha Ka Ree and a very powerful lifeform trapped at the center of the galaxy.

episodes.title: Star Trek V: The Final Frontier

events.summary: Hikaru Sulu assumes command of the USS Excelsior.

James T. Kirk, Spock, and Leonard McCoy visit Yosemite National Park for their shore leave. Kirk nearly dies from falling off of El Capitan while climbing it, but is saved by Spock and his levitation boots.

episodes.title: Star Trek V: The Final Frontier

events.summary: Spock's half-brother, the renegade Vulcan Sybok captures the Federation, Klingon, and Romulan ambassadors on Nimbus III.

episodes.title: Star Trek V: The Final Frontier

events.summary: The Lancelot starships are introduced into Starfleet service. One of the first ships of this class, the USS Leondegrance, is launched.
Negotiations between the Federation and the Klingon Empire take place on the Korvat colony. At the time, Curzon Dax was representing the Federation, while Kang represented the Klingons.

T'Meni and her husband, both of Vulcan, send their son Tuvok to Starfleet Academy, as it was their wish that he become a Starfleet officer. Feeling obliged to honor his parent's wishes Tuvok consents to attend.
Hikaru Sulu begins a three-year mission charting gaseous anomalies in the Beta Quadrant.

episodes.title: Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country

events.summary: The Klingon battle cruiser IKS T'Ong is sent by the Klingon High Command on a high priority mission. For this mission, the crew is placed into cryogenic sleep.

Three Klingon battle cruisers, commanded by Kor, Koloth, and Kang, are dispatched to apprehend the Albino. The raid is successful in breaking up most of the Albino's power base, but they fail to capture the Albino himself. In revenge, the Albino infects each of the three captain's firstborn sons with a genetic virus that will slowly kill the children.
By this year, Klingons and Romulans regard each other as "blood enemies".

Doing his part to save the Enterprise-B from being destroyed by the Nexus on her maiden voyage, Captain James Tiberius Kirk, one of the most celebrated Starfleet officers in history, is believed to have been killed.
Starfleet opens up a classified database on the Borg, likely due to the contact with the El-Aurians, a species that has nearly been wiped out by the Borg, and the mysterious incident in 2153.

The Excelsior is struck by a powerful subspace shock wave caused by the destruction of the Klingon moon, Praxis. Upon discovering the cause of the disaster, Sulu immediately contacted the Klingon High Command to offer his ship's assistance. The Klingons, including an officer on Praxis, called for help, however, the transmission was cut off when Brigadier Kerla interrupted and reported that there had been an incident on Praxis, and turned the offer for assistance down because everything is under control, requesting that Sulu obey treaty stipulations and remain outside the Neutral Zone. Thereafter, Sulu reported the incident to Starfleet Command.

episodes.title: Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country

events.summary: The Khitomer Conference takes place, paving the way for nearly a hundred years of peace between the Klingon Empire and the Federation. Curzon Dax is one of the Federation diplomats present who negotiates the Accords.

episodes.title: Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country

events.summary: The Klingon moon Praxis explodes.

episodes.title: Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country

events.summary: The Nexus; a powerful, destructive energy-ribbon and inter-dimensional portal is discovered by the Federation for the first time with the crew of the Enterprise-B. It claims the lives of hundreds of El-Aurian refugees aboard the SS Lakul and SS Robert Fox which were both destroyed by the ribbon.
The USS Enterprise-A is decommissioned.

episodes.title: Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country

events.summary: The USS Enterprise is commissioned under the command of Captain John Harriman.
Tuvok graduates from Starfleet Academy and is assigned to the starship USS Excelsior, under the command of Captain Sulu.

USS Enterprise departs the Spacedock One to rendezvous with Kronos One. Captain James T. Kirk is framed for the assassination of Klingon Chancellor Gorkon. He and Dr. Leonard McCoy are given life sentences and sent to the dilithium mines of Rura Penthe but are both rescued by Captain Spock and the crew of the Enterprise-A.

episodes.title: Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country

events.summary: A northern continent on Oby VI is afflicted by a plasma plague. Dr. Susan Nuress, the principal investigator, and her associates are sent by the Federation to find a cure. The initial cure proves ineffective.

The USS Jenolan, while en route to the retirement colony on Norpin V, encounters a Dyson sphere. While the Jenolan’s captain investigates the sphere, the ship becomes trapped in its gravity well and crashes. The only two survivors, Ensign Matt Franklin and passenger Captain Montgomery Scott, unable to wait for a rescue, place themselves in suspended animation by storing their patterns in the pattern buffer of the ship's transporter.
Doctor Susan Nuress continues her pioneering research on plasma plague following a second epidemic on Oby VI. She proves successful in isolating a cure for the victims of the plague.
On Rutia IV, the eastern continent denies the western continent independence, an event considered to be the start of the Ansata movement.
A Klingon expedition makes first contact with the Ventaxians.
Tuvok resigns from Starfleet and begins the process of the Kolinahr ritual.
The USS Leondegrance, under the command of Captain Nyota Uhura, begins an exploratory five-year mission of the Lesser Magellanic Cloud.
A Federation vessel visits Angel I, reporting the native civilization had reached the technological equivalent of Earth's mid-20th century.
Jaresh-Inyo enters politics.
Juliana O'Donnell is born.
Upon entering pon farr, Tuvok leaves the discipline of Kolinahr and marries T'Pel.
July 13th – Jean-Luc Picard is born in La Barre, France.
Susan Beaumont is born.
The USS Leondegrance, under the command of Captain Nyota Uhura, ends its exploratory five-year mission of the Lesser Magellanic Cloud.
Theodore Brahms is born.
Timicin is born on Kaelon II.
Indri VIII is first identified by a Federation vessel.
Joseph Sisko is born.
The Cardassians arrive at Bajor to "help" the Bajorans, ultimately leading to the Occupation of Bajor.

A conflict between the Federation and the Romulan Star Empire, known as the Tomed Incident takes place, the outcome of which leads to the Treaty of Algeron. The treaty redefines the Romulan Neutral Zone, and as well, ends all Federation research into cloaking technology. Afterward, the Romulans go into a period of complete isolation.
Jillur Gueta is born on Bajor.
The planet Meridian appears in the primary universe. It does not appear again until 2371.
During a raid on an Acamarian outpost, the Gatherer Penthor-Mul, a member of the Lornak clan is captured and imprisoned. Before he can be tried, he is murdered by Yuta, a member of the Tralesta clan.
Kevin Uxbridge marries Rishon.
Kira Meru is born on Bajor.

episodes.title: Wrongs Darker Than Death or Night

events.summary: Mark Jameson marries Anne.
The USS Stratford under the command of Captain A. Williams is destroyed. Starfleet Intelligence suspects this was orchestrated by the Minosian High Council.
The USS Leondegrance, under the command of Captain Nyota Uhura, is assigned to Starfleet Academy where it becomes a training vessel.

Karnas takes sixty-three passengers hostage aboard a starliner on Mordan IV. Commander Mark Jameson secures their release by supplying weapons to Karnas, precipitating forty years of civil war on Mordan.
Surmak Ren is born on Bajor to Surmak Hoek and Surmak Stimson.
T'Lyn is born.
Lewis Zimmerman is born on Earth to Gregory Zimmerman and Sandra Fritz.
Lisa Cusak is born.
Miranda Vigo is born on New Gaul.
The USS Excelsior is decommissioned.
Timor Landi is born on Bajor.
Boothby begins his career as groundskeeper at Starfleet Academy.
Stefan is born.
Jean-Luc Picard applies to Starfleet Academy but is rejected.
Raymond Marr is born.
April – Picard participates in the forty-first Starfleet Academy marathon on Danula II and becomes the first freshman to ever win the marathon.

episodes.title: The Best of Both Worlds, Part II

events.summary: Jean-Luc Picard's second application to Starfleet Academy is successful.
Beverly Howard is born in Copernicus City on Luna.

The parrises squares team of the Starfleet Academy wins the championship finale against the highly favored team of Minsk. The following party in the Academy grounds causes Boothby three weeks of work repairing the damage.
The Starfleet training ship USS Republic returns to the Sol system. It does not leave the system for the next fifty years.
2 February – Devinoni Ral is born in Brussels, European Alliance on Earth.
The Son'a subdue the Tarlac and the Ellora and integrate them into their society as a labor class.
The USS Stargazer is commissioned and enters into Starfleet service.
After his graduation, Ensign Jean-Luc Picard has to be implanted with an artificial heart after he is seriously wounded in a fight with a Nausicaan over a game of dom-jot at Starbase Earhart.
Cadet Jean-Luc Picard joins the Speed of Light Club, after flying three hundred thousand kilometers per second in level flight aboard the cruiser USS Leondegrance.
Ensign Cortan Zweller is assigned to the USS Ajax under Captain Narth.
The Class of 2327 graduates from Starfleet Academy. Its members included Jean-Luc Picard, Marta Batanides, and Cortan Zweller. Picard has a degree in Command and Control.
Timicin begins research on how to save his homeworld's sun.
William Samuels is born in Bergen, Norway on Earth.
Doctor Noonien Soong marries Juliana O'Donnell on Mavala IV.
Ishan Chaye is born on Bajor.
Lwaxana Troi gets a new valet, Mr. Xelo.
Miles O'Brien is born in Ireland to Michael O'Brien during September of this year.
Starfleet Lieutenant Ian Andrew Troi and the Betazoid Ambassador Lwaxana Troi are married.
The Cardassian Union begins "to help and develop" Bajor, starting its de facto occupation.
Arjin's father becomes a pilot instructor at the Gedana post.
Chakotay is born on Trebus near what becomes the Cardassian Demilitarized Zone.
Kestra Troi, the first daughter of Lwaxana and Ian Andrew Troi, is born on Betazed.
Marc Brooks is born.
Mullibok stows away aboard a Cardassian survey vessel and escapes Bajor to one of its moons, Jeraddo.
Starfleet begins to adopt a new system of computing using isolinear optical chips to replace the aging duotronic systems.
Thomas Raymond is born in Indianapolis, Indiana.
The future Doctor Kingsley is born.
August – Joseph and Sarah get married following a two-month courtship.
Boradis III is the first planet in the Boradis system colonized by the Federation. Over the next thirty-four years, three other settlements will appear in this star system.
June – Joseph Sisko meets a woman named Sarah in Jackson Square.
An eight-year old Beverly Howard has her first crush on a eleven-year old soccer player named Stefan, which led to her having fantasies about having children, a career, and a happy life.
Benjamin Sisko, the son of Joseph and Sarah Sisko, is born in New Orleans, Louisiana on Earth.
The impulse engines later used aboard the Val Jean are built.
The Nexus returns to the Milky Way Galaxy thirty-nine years after James Kirk was taken by it.
Captain Jean-Luc Picard is named as a honorary Olympian at the 12th annual Starfleet Academy Games.
Captain Nyota Uhura retires from Starfleet.

During a historic battle involving the Constellation starship USS Stargazer and attacking hostile forces, the captain is killed and Lieutenant Commander Jean-Luc Picard takes command of the bridge. In recognition of his service, Picard is promoted to permanent commanding officer of the vessel.
Gaila begins a forty-year career in the weapons-dealing business.
Sarah Sisko disappears two days after Benjamin Sisko's first birthday.
August 19th – William T. Riker is born in Alaska on Earth.
Chakotay, at the age of six, aspires to become a paleontologist.
February 16th – Geordi La Forge is born in Mogadishu, Somalia on Earth.
L. Isao Telaka is born.
Lore is activated on Omicron Theta.
According to Felisa Howard's journals, Ronin was 34 years old in 2370 and would have been born in 2336, though his actual age was much older.
Data is built on Omicron Theta, and begins his endeavor to become more Human.
Doctor Noonien Soong and Juliana Soong relocate their laboratory amid the jungle of Terlina III.

In the Delta Quadrant, the Nokaro, a colony ship carrying nearly three thousand people, mostly families, is consumed by a giant bioplasmic organism, which fools the crew into believing that they are headed for an inhabitable planet. Qatai's family is among those lost, leading him to dedicate his life to destroying the creature.
Kestra Troi dies in a drowning accident in Lake El'nar on Betazed.
March 29th – Deanna Troi is born near Lake El'nar on Betazed.
Sarah Sisko dies in a hovercraft accident. Her former husband, Joseph, discovers Sarah's whereabouts but also discovers that she had died a month earlier.
September 11th – Leah Brahms is born in Damascus City on Alpha Delphi IX.
The USS Leondegrance is decomissioned.
Beverly Howard dyes her hair brunette, but quickly changes it back.
Natasha Yar is born on Turkana IV.
The colony on Turkana IV begins falling apart, with dozens of factions fighting for control of Turkana City.
Trill Ambassador Odan negotiates a peace between the Alpha and Beta moons of Peliar Zel.
William T. Riker's mother, Betty, dies.
A landing party from the USS Tripoli discovers the android Data amid the remains of the Omicron Theta colony. He is activated on February 2 of this year.
Greta Vanderweg is born in the Alpha Centauri system.
Mabus is born on the Trabe homeworld.
The Omicron Theta colony is destroyed by the Crystalline Entity. Raymond Marr is among the casualties.
Curzon Dax works as a mediator in finding a resolution to the Klaestron Civil War.
General Ardelon Tandro was murdered.
Jean-Luc Picard last sees his archaeology professor, Doctor Galen. At this time in his life, Picard is considering a career in this field of science.
Sev Maylor is born.
According to the con artist Alsia, her stellar cartographer father, conducts a mineralogical study of the Vlugta asteroid belt and finds it rich with minerals.
At age five, starts school and begins to tell his new friends the same stories about his mother that his father told him.
Captain Jean-Luc Picard is awarded the Hall of Honor for his outstanding work ethic by the United Federation Planets.
Edward M. La Forge and his wife Silva La Forge save their son Geordi La Forge when he is caught in a fire.

Lwaxana Troi deletes the journal files from 2329 to a few months after her daughter Deanna Troi's birth in 2336. These files contain references to her first daughter, Kestra Troi, who had died tragically in 2336.
Nathaniel Teros develops the theory of neuromuscular adaptation for low-gravity species; however, at the time, it has no practical success.
Ro Laren is born on Bajor to Ro Talia and Ro Gale.
The Yaderans are conquered by the Dominion.
Worf, son of Mogh, is born on Qo'noS.
According to a holonovel called Crisis Point II: Paradoxus, the Great Soolian Algae Crisis happened this year, though this may or may not have been fictional.
Data is permitted to enter Starfleet Academy.
Jadzia is born on Trill.
Julian Bashir is born on Earth.
Lwaxana Troi first uses the nickname "Little One" for her daughter Deanna.
9 April – Jean-Luc Picard has a rendezvous with Jenice at the Café des Artistes in Paris, which he fails to keep.
After one of the referees calls a penalty against his team during the championship finals, Klingon parrises squares player M'Kota R'Cho strangles him.
Beverly Howard is admitted to Starfleet Academy's medical program.
Ishara Yar is born on Turkana IV.
Lewis Zimmerman graduates from Starfleet Academy.
Natasha Yar and Ishara become orphans following the death of their parents and enter the custody of their guardians, who later abandon them. Natasha begins to sustain them both.
Sneed is born on Ferenginar.
Surmak Ren graduates as a medical doctor from the University of Bajor.
An eight year old Geordi La Forge is given his first pet, a Circassian cat.
Ian Andrew Troi, father of Deanna, dies.
Kira Nerys is born on Bajor.

episodes.title: Wrongs Darker Than Death or Night

events.summary: Kyle Riker teaches his son, William, anbo-jyutsu.
A Starfleet captain fails in his attempted contact with the Jarada following a protocol error that causes a twenty-year rift between the two groups.
Annika Hansen is born on the Tendara colony.
Chakotay's entry into Starfleet Academy is sponsored by Captain Sulu.
Devinoni Ral leaves Earth and relocates to Hurkos III.

In an alternate timeline created by a temporal rift, the Enterprise-C disappears while engaged with the Romulan attackers. It crosses into the year 2366 (an alternate version of the prime timeline), which is then altered through unknown means. The ship returns through the rift with Natasha Yar. In the Battle of Narendra III, the USS Enterprise-C is destroyed while defending the Klingon outpost on Narendra III from Romulan attack. However, some of the Enterprise crew survives and are captured, among them Natasha Yar, who had been sent there by Jean-Luc Picard from 2366, in an attempt to fix the unexplained alteration of memories and the appearance of the Enterprise.

In what becomes known as the Battle of Narendra III, the USS Enterprise-C is destroyed while defending the Klingon outpost on Narendra III from Romulan attack. This event reemphasizes the Khitomer Accords and ensures a lasting peace between the Federation and the Klingon Empire.
Kyle Riker and his son William go on a fishing trip in Alaska.
Roana and her husband open a shop on Bajor.
Shakaar Edon begins to fight the Cardassians during their Occupation of Bajor.
The Enterprise-C responds to a distress call from the Klingon outpost on Narendra III. Concurrently, the Klingon Empire and the Federation are negotiating a peace treaty.
At the age of 17, Miles O'Brien joins Starfleet as an enlisted crewman and is assigned to the USS Rutledge under the command of Benjamin Maxwell.
Data graduates from Starfleet Academy and is promoted to ensign.

In the Delta Quadrant, the Kazon band together and overthrow the Trabe. They steal a large number of Trabe warships, force the Trabe from their homeworld, and head out into space. Soon after this, the Kazon revert to fighting among themselves.

Kor, while serving on the Klingon Oversight Council, strikes the name Martok off of the Klingon Defense Force's candidate list after noticing that he comes from the lowlands of Ketha Province. Martok later signs on aboard General ShiVang's flagship as a civilian laborer.
Kurn, brother to Worf, is born on Qo'noS to Mogh and his wife.
Michael O'Brien sends a recorded audition of his son Miles playing the cello to the Aldebaran Music Academy.
Odo assumes humanoid form for the first time.
Sela is born on Romulus to her mother Natasha Yar after Tasha became the consort of a Romulan general.
The Albino comes out of hiding, following a narrow escape from Kang, Koloth, Kor, and Curzon Dax on Galdonterre, and establishes a fortified compound on Secarus IV.
According to an alternate Jean-Luc Picard of 2366, a war between the Federation and Klingon began in this year.
After the Khitomer Massacre, the Intrepid pays a visit to Starbase 24 where another survivor, Kahlest, disembarks for medical treatment. She later books a passage to her home in the Klingon Empire.
At the age of 5, Julian Bashir performs his first surgery, on his teddy bear, Kukalaka.

In what is later known as the Khitomer Massacre, the Klingon colony on Khitomer is attacked and destroyed by Romulan forces. Among the casualties is Mogh, whose son Worf survives and is later rescued by the Intrepid.
Jean-Luc Picard has an affair with Miranda Vigo on Earth. Shortly after they break up, Miranda becomes involved with another man, also in Starfleet, with whom she has a son named Jason the next year.
Paul Stubbs begins to research and construct "The Egg" to study the explosion of the neutron star/red giant in Kavis Alpha sector.
