Several Bajoran women, including Kira Meru and her daughter, Kira Nerys (who traveled back in time from 2374), are brought to Terok Nor to serve as companions to the Cardassian military officers. Meru is chosen as his personal mistress by the new prefect, Gul Dukat. She remains in this capacity until her death in 2353.

episodes.title: Wrongs Darker Than Death or Night

events.summary: The Bajoran Resistance attempts to kill Gul Dukat by planting a bomb in his quarters on Terok Nor.

episodes.title: Wrongs Darker Than Death or Night

events.summary: The new Cardassian ore processing center orbiting Bajor, Terok Nor, nears completion.

episodes.title: Wrongs Darker Than Death or Night

events.summary: Dr. Paul Manheim begins a search on a location that meets his criteria for his time and gravity experiments.
Jason Vigo is born.
Jean-Luc Picard pays a visit to his hometown La Barre, France.
Kira Nerys demonstrates she has no skill in fingerpainting.
Miles O'Brien becomes a transporter chief.
Ro Gale is killed during a Cardassian interrogation while his daughter Ro Laren is forced to watch.

The Hansen family say their good-byes, and depart the Drexler outpost aboard the USS Raven on their mission to study the Borg. They do not file a flight plan, and no further contact with the Raven is made after their departure.
The Setlik III massacre takes place, in which Cardassian forces raid the Federation colony on the planet.
To keep his son William interested and challenged in anbo-jytsu, Kyle Riker cheats at the martial arts during combat.
Worf goes to live on Gault with his adopted parents Sergey and Helena Rozhenko and his foster brother, Nikolai. The seven year old, also at some point, gets into a fight with teenagers at school.

episodes.title: Let He Who Is Without Sin...

events.summary: Zek wins the Global Tongo Championship, beginning a streak that will last until at least 2373.
Beverly Howard marries Jack R. Crusher.
Chakotay graduates from Starfleet Academy.
Enabran Tain becomes head of the Obsidian Order.
Ensign Data is promoted to lieutenant.
Jack R. Crusher records a holographic message to his newborn son Wesley.
Julian Bashir undergoes a DNA resequencing treatment on Adigeon Prime, thereby improving his intelligence and coordination abilities.
The last attempt for reconciliation between Acamar III and the Gatherers prior to 2366 fails.
The Romulans begin a 20-year effort at destroying the Klingon-Federation Alliance.
Wesley Crusher is born on Earth.
Zek wins the Global Tongo Championship for the second year in a row.
Captain Jean-Luc Picard is awarded the Silver Medal in Diplomacy by the allies of the Federation.
Dr. Paul Manheim assembles a team of scientists and heads off to find a suitable location for his experiments on time and gravity.
Federation-Klingon relations around this year are described as "not too cordial".
Harry Kim is born in South Carolina on Earth.
Lyndsay Ballard is born.
On Romulus, Natasha Yar tries to escape together with her daughter Sela. However, the little girl cries out as her mother takes her away from her home and Yar is subsequently executed.

Salia is born on Daled IV, her parents both die shortly after her birth. An unnamed Federation starship later takes Salia and her governess, Anya, from Daled IV to Klavdia III, so that Salia could be raised in a neutral environment.
Starfleet officer Ensign DeSeve defects to the Romulan Star Empire and begins to live on Romulus, where he will stay for the following twenty years.
Tuvok returns to Starfleet and serves aboard the USS Wyoming.
Ty Kajada begins tracking down the Kobliad criminal Rao Vantika.
Zek wins the Global Tongo Championship for the third year in a row.
A group of Native Americans settle on the planet Dorvan V near the Cardassian border.
Annika Hansen is assimilated by the Borg.
At the age of fifteen, William T. Riker is abandoned by his father, Kyle Riker.
Benjamin Sisko and Solok enter Starfleet Academy.

episodes.title: Take Me Out to the Holosuite

events.summary: Beverly Crusher graduates from Starfleet Medical Academy. ( biographical screen) Consequently, visits with her grandmother, Felisa Howard drop off significantly.
Captain Jean-Luc Picard receives the Betazoid Loyalties award for his work in assisting developing worlds.
Divok is born on Qo'noS.
Dr. Telek R'Mor begins a three-year mission conducting "secret research" aboard the Romulan science vessel Talvath.
Proconsul Neral's immediate family are killed in a Klingon raid.

Romulan forces make a foolhardy attempt to board Klingon General ShiVang's flagship. Following the ensuing battle, Martok, a civilian laborer, is given a battlefield commission in the Klingon Defense Forces.
William Samuels moves to the Federation colony on Volan II.
Zek wins the Global Tongo Championship for the fourth year in a row.
Amanda Rogers is born in Kansas in North America as daughter of two Qs in Human form.
B'Elanna Torres' father, John Torres, leaves her mother Miral.
Benjamin Sisko serves as captain of the Academy wrestling team.
Captain Jean-Luc Picard is awarded the Humanitarian Award of Federation Planets by the United Federation of Planets.
Carolyn L. Raymond is born on Earth.
Construction of mining station Terok Nor is completed.

episodes.title: Wrongs Darker Than Death or Night

events.summary: Keena and Baltrim escaped the Cardassian Occupation of Bajor and found shelter on Mullibok's cottage on the Bajoran moon Jerrado.
Lurak T'Luco is born on Romulus.
Mardah is born on Bajor.

On Terok Nor, Bajoran scientists Dekon Elig and Surmak Ren plant an aphasia device, created to kill the Cardassians on the station with an aphasia virus. However, the device is never activated and lies dormant until 2369.
Quark leaves his home on Ferenginar after reaching the Age of Ascension.
Richard Bashir is a Federation diplomat on Invernia II. His son Julian Bashir, after witnessing the death of an Invernian girl, decides to become a doctor.
The Duras sisters' Bird-of-Prey is commissioned in this year.
Vandor IV is selected by Dr. Paul Manheim as the location for his time experiments.
While on a three-year science mission, the Romulan Telek R'Mor is contacted by the USS Voyager from the year 2371 via a micro-wormhole.
Zek wins the Global Tongo Championship for the fifth year in a row.
According to a falsified Starfleet personnel file, Kieran MacDuff enters Starfleet Academy.
Beverly Crusher interns on Delos IV under Doctor Dalen Quaice.
Ishara Yar becomes a Turkana IV Coalition member.
Joseph Sisko's family doctor moves to New Orleans.
Natasha Yar flees from the failed Human colony on Turkana IV.

episodes.title: Where No One Has Gone Before

events.summary: Sneed is arrested on Ferenginar. He is found guilty of falsifying documents and sentenced to pay 5 bars of gold pressed latinum.
Tim Watters is born.
Turkana IV severs relations with the Federation.
Varria partners with Kivas Fajo.
Zek wins the Global Tongo Championship for the sixth year in a row.
David, a genetically-engineered Human, is born at the Darwin Genetic Research Station.
Hector Ilario is born.
In the Delta Quadrant, five members of Viorsa's species go into hibernation as the planet they live on is rendered uninhabitable by a solar flare.

Jack R. Crusher dies on an away mission. Jean-Luc Picard, Crusher's captain aboard the Stargazer, delivers the sad news to Jack's wife, Beverly Crusher. Following his death Beverly packs up some things, placing them in storage, including a holographic message Jack recorded for his son Wesley in 2348 when Wesley was about ten weeks old. She doesn't remove the small container from storage until 2367.
Jeremiah Rossa is born at Galen IV colony.
Kira Meru dies in a Cardassian hospital.

episodes.title: Wrongs Darker Than Death or Night

events.summary: One of the childrenwho? living on Deep Space 9 in 2369 was born in this year.
Raffaela Musiker is born on Earth.
The Tholians destroy a Federation starbase. The only survivor of the attack is Kyle Riker.
Tora Ziyal, daughter of Gul Dukat and Tora Naprem, is born.
William T. Riker and Geordi La Forge enter Starfleet Academy in San Francisco, Earth.
Worf accidentally kills a boy named Mikel in a soccer match.

episodes.title: Let He Who Is Without Sin...

events.summary: Zek wins the Global Tongo Championship for the seventh year in a row.

Benjamin Sisko and Solok graduate from Starfleet Academy. Sisko meets his future wife, Jennifer at Gilgo Beach, shortly after graduation. Later that year, they both attend the Mazurka Festival in New Berlin together with their friends Cal and Gretchen Hudson.

episodes.title: Take Me Out to the Holosuite

events.summary: Doctor T'Pan becomes director of the Vulcan Science Academy.
Jack B. Raymond is born on Earth.
Jeremy Aster is born on Earth.
Kathryn Janeway plays her last game of tennis. She does not play again for another nineteen years.
The USS Stargazer visits the world Chalna.
Zek wins the Global Tongo Championship for the eighth year in a row.
Captain Jean-Luc Picard is court martialed for the loss of the Stargazer. The prosecutor for Starfleet Command is Phillipa Louvois.
Chu'lak is assigned to the Strata as an ensign.
Deanna Troi enters Starfleet Academy.
Dr. Paul Manheim first devises his theory that if the consistency of time is altered, a doorway into another dimension will appear.

In what later became known as the Battle of Maxia, the USS Stargazer is attacked by an unknown vessel (later revealed to be Ferengi in origin). His ship severely damaged, Captain Jean-Luc Picard devises a maneuver that makes the Stargazer appear in two places at once, which allows him to destroy the alien vessel. This maneuver, accomplished by making a very short warp jump, is dubbed the "Picard Maneuver". Picard and his crew are subsequently forced to abandon the stricken Stargazer, limping through space in shuttlecraft for weeks before being rescued.
Jake Sisko is born, son of Benjamin and Jennifer Sisko.
Jean-Luc Picard receives the Klingon Planetary Humanitarian Award.
John and Mary Kim take their son Harry to a haunted house.
Kira Nerys joins the Shakaar Edon base camp in the hopes of becoming a resistance member.
Seven of Nine emerges from a Borg maturation chamber, having been assimilated five years before.
Worf completes his first Rite of Ascension.
Worf visits the Great Domes of Qo'noS for the first time.
Zek wins the Global Tongo Championship for the ninth year in a row.
Abaddon begins maintaining his inventory for Abaddon's Repository of Lost Treasures.
According to a falsified Starfleet personnel file, Kieran MacDuff graduates from Starfleet Academy.

In the Delta Quadrant, the Talaxian moon Rinax is attacked by a metreon cascade engineered by the Haakonian scientist Ma'Bor Jetrel. The moon is rendered uninhabitable, killing hundreds of thousands of Talaxians.

Julian Bashir discovers that he was genetically enhanced and believes that his parents might have considered him to have been defective. He stops using their nickname for him, "Jules", in favor of "Julian".
Kathryn Janeway meets Tuvok for the first time.
Kira Nerys becomes a member of the Shakaar resistance cell.
One of William and Louise Samuels' two daughters is born on Volan II.
Production of Mark V transporters is stopped.
Surmak Ren is arrested several times but released for insufficient evidence.

The Alcyone people destroy a Tarellian vessel which is assumed to be the last ship of this ill-fated species. Up to the year 2364, the Tarellian people are thought of as extinct until a lone starship entered the Haven star system.
Zek wins the Global Tongo Championship for the tenth year in a row.
As part of the Galen border conflict, the Talarians attack the Federation colony on Galen IV, where the four-year-old Jeremiah Rossa loses his parents and is adopted by the Talarian officer Endar.
Geordi La Forge is assigned to the Victory following his graduation from Starfleet Academy.
In the Delta Quadrant, a Hirogen is captured by Penk and is forced to fight in Tsunkatse matches.
Kathryn M. Janeway graduated from Starfleet Academy.
Surmak Ren is convicted of terrorist activity by the Cardassian Regional Court on charges of terrorist activity stemming from the kidnapping of Gul Spumco.
T'Jon, a native of Ornara in the Delos system, assumes command of the interplanetary freighter Sanction.
The Cardassian Gallitep labor camp is liberated by the Shakaar resistance cell.
The freighter Odin is declared missing after a collision with an asteroid. After five months in escape pods, the survivors land on Angel I.
Vash visits Earth for the last time.
Will Riker graduated from Starfleet Academy, eighth in his graduating class.
William T. Riker receives his first assignment to the Pegasus as a helmsman.
Worf enters Starfleet Academy.
Zek wins the Global Tongo Championship for the eleventh year in a row.

Harry Kim joins his parents, John and Mary, on a humanitarian mission to a colony that had just suffered from a radiation disaster. While there, the Kims visit a hospital, and Harry wanders off by himself to an area where he wasn't supposed to be. In this area, he sees many sick and dying, including a little girl on an operating table. As he watches, the doctor calls for a scalpel, and the little girl looks at Harry, her face filled with fear. As well, at some point this year, the nine year old Kim contracts the Mendakan pox.
In the Delta Quadrant, the "Think Tank" group recruits an unnamed person into their number. They do not attempt to do so again until 2375, when they extend the offer to Seven of Nine.
Miranda Vigo and her son, Jason, move to a colony on the planet Camor V.
One of William and Louise Samuels' two daughters is born on Volan II.
Ro Laren enters Starfleet Academy.
Rugal is born.
Tanis is born.
The daughter of Lieutenant Onara, an officer in the Bajoran militia, is born on Bajor.
The Parada civil war begins.

The Pegasus is presumed destroyed after an apparent warp core breach. The ship had been a test bed for a new phasing cloaking device developed by Starfleet Intelligence, and the ship's crew had mutinied in protest against the creation of such a device. In fact, the ship, still in a phased state, drifts through space, eventually dephasing inside an asteroid in the Devolin system. Following the Judge Advocate General's inquiry into the incident, William T. Riker is assigned to Betazed.
Vice Admiral Janeway, father of Kathryn Janeway, drowns beneath the ice cap of Tau Ceti Prime.
Willie Potts is born.
Zek wins the Global Tongo Championship for the twelfth year in a row.
Admiral Mark Jameson is diagnosed with Iverson's Disease.
After forty years, the civil war on Mordan IV ends, with Karnas emerging as governor.
Deanna Troi graduates from Starfleet Academy.
Dr. Bathkin dies in a shuttlecraft accident.
Kar is born.
Piri is born.
William T. Riker begins practicing "Night Bird" on his trombone.
Worf and K'Ehleyr separate because neither feel ready to engage in a deep relationship.
Zek wins the Global Tongo Championship for the thirteenth year in a row.
Alixus deliberately arranges for the SS Santa Maria to be stranded on a planet in the Orellius system, in order to establish her own technology-free colony.
Before the Cardassians hit the Lunar V base, the Bajoran Resistance hid a few of their sub-impulse raiders underground.
Captain Jean-Luc Picard receives an award from the Galaxy of Planets and Suns fundraiser.
Darrell Oja Raymond is born to Thomas and Louise Cara Raymond.
Due to the effects of Iverson's Disease, Admiral Mark Jameson is confined to a support chair.
Galis learns that Dekon Elig is imprisoned at the Velos VII internment camp.
Gul Dukat is assigned command of Terok Nor.
In a small village on Bajor, Hovath becomes the apprentice to the sirah in order to succeed him.
In the Delta Quadrant, the wife of Caylem, a member of the Alsaurian resistance movement against the Mokra Order, dies in prison.
Jono receives the first of his many injuries. Over the next seven years, he will experience a broken arm, two fractured ribs, and a low-grade concussion.
Li Nalas, a Bajoran Resistance fighter and former Terok Nor miner, is captured by the Cardassians and presumed dead. He spends the next ten years at the Hutet labor camp on Cardassia IV.
Mekor, the son of Dukat, is born.
Sarina Douglas begins to live with Jack, Lauren, and Patrick.
Taya is "born" on Yadera II.
The son of Dara, grandson of Timicin is born on Kaelon II.
The Velara Base on Velara III begins its terraforming of the planet.
Vreenak becomes a key member of the Romulan Senate.
Zek wins the Global Tongo Championship for the fourteenth year in a row.
Botanists Kevin and Rishon Uxbridge move from New Martim Vaz on Earth to the Federation colony on Rana IV.
Dr. Dalen Quaice is assigned to the starbase. He moves there with his significant other, Patricia.
Lewis Zimmerman is assigned to the Holo-Programming Center on the station. He begins developing what he refers to as a "Holo-Doc": a holographic doctor intended to assist in medical emergencies.
Lidell marries Tolen Ren on Banea.
Lieutenant William T. Riker is promoted to lieutenant commander for "exceptional valor during the evacuation of the research station on Nervala IV."
Obsidian Order operative Iliana Ghemor volunteers for an undercover assignment on Bajor. She is surgically altered to appear as a Bajoran and infiltrate the Resistance. Her memories are erased.
One of the children living on Deep Space 9 in 2369 was born in this year.
Roana and her husband, both Bajoran entrepreneurs, open a store on the Promenade of Terok Nor, after closing down their shop on Bajor after seventeen years.
Rom leaves Ferenginar.
The father of Jeremy Aster dies from a Rushton infection.
The Potemkin attempts to contact the failed Federation colony on Turkana IV. They are warned that anyone beaming down to the planet will be killed.

The Potemkin sends down an away team, led by Lieutenant William T. Riker to evacuate a research station on Nervala IV. Due to atmospheric distortion, the Potemkin transporter chief attempts to use two confinement beams to transport Lieutenant Riker, the last one on the planet. Although the first confinement beam works and a Riker is beamed aboard, the second beam is unknowingly reflected back to the planet, creating another William T. Riker. The second Riker would later be discovered by the crew of the USS Enterprise in 2369.
The Shakaar resistance cell, including a young Kira Nerys, spends the entire winter hiding in the Dahkur Hills from Cardassian sensor sweeps.
Vash is hired as the personal assistant to Dr. Samuel Estragon, a scientist devoted to finding the legendary Tox Uthat.
William T. Riker and Deanna Troi meet for the first time on Betazed while Riker is stationed there as a lieutenant.
Zek wins the Global Tongo Championship for the fifteenth year in a row.
Beverly Crusher is promoted to commander after passing the bridge officer examination.
Cadet Ro Laren graduates from Starfleet Academy.
Dekon Elig is killed by phaser fire on Velos VII.

Ensign Marla Finn begins a romantic relationship with Walter Pierce. She soon begins an affair with William Hodges and both are killed by a jealous Pierce in a plasma explosion. Afterward, Pierce vaporizes himself in the Enterprise’s plasma stream. His Betazoid empathic trace is inadvertently left in the nacelle tube bulkhead.
Fallit Kot hijacks a shipment of Romulan ale. He is caught and sentenced to eight years in a labor camp.

In an alternate timeline in which the USS Enterprise disappeared mysteriously in 2344. Natasha Yar is assigned to the first Galaxy battleship, the USS Enterprise following her graduation from Starfleet Academy.
O'Brien is transferred to the operations division and promoted to tactical officer.

On Mordan IV, construction workers use steelplast for the sealing off of segments of tunnels under the principal city of the planet. An example of a tunnel segment sealed from adjoining tunnels is the subsidiary tunnel M-4.
Quark loans his cousin Gaila an amount of latinum to start up a munitions consortium. Gaila promises Quark to pay him back in a shuttle should he become a success.
Raymond Boone is killed. He is replaced by a Cardassian spy and returned to Starfleet. Within the year, "Boone" resigns from Starfleet, leaves his wife, and moves to a Cardassian border planet.

Rugal, son of Kotan Pa'Dar, is kidnapped by a Cardassian officer and taken to the Tozhat Resettlement Center after his mother is killed in a Bajoran Resistance attack. He would be adopted by Bajoran Proka Migdal.
Starfleet reports Marla E. Finn and William Hodges as missing.
The OSHC investigation into Finn's disappearance is terminated with no result.
The Setlik III massacre.
The Victory investigates the mysterious disappearance of 49 people on the Tarchannen III outpost. Ensign Geordi La Forge is involved in the away team.
Under the command of Captain Blackwood, the Tombaugh is attacked and assimilated by the Borg. Among the crew is Ensign Stone, fresh out of Starfleet Academy.
Warp engine control systems are installed on the USS Enterprise.
Zek wins the Global Tongo Championship for the sixteenth year in a row.
Admiral Norah Satie begins a four-year period of being stationed at planets and starbase as she works on the cause of preserving the Federation.
Artim is born on the Ba'ku planet.
Construction of the Enterprise at Utopia Planitia is completed. Commander Quinteros, who supervised the construction, would later accept a post at Starbase 74.
Eight months before her destruction in 2364, this ship is assigned to study the collapse of a red supergiant star into a white dwarf.
Eli Foster is born on Earth.
George Primmin becomes a security officer.
Greta Vanderweg begins her Starfleet service.
Gul Darhe'el dies in his sleep due to a coleibric hemorrhage.
Guls Dukat and Hadar attend a reception at the Bajoran Center for Science to view Odo and his abilities. The Changeling performs the Cardassian "neck trick" to wide acclaim.

In his search for the Albino, Dahar master Kang discovers one of the Albino's discarded wives on Dayos IV. Kang fed and clothed her and later told her the story of his murdered son and his quest. Although he suspected that she knew the location, she said nothing.
Jean-Luc Picard assumes command of the Enterprise.

Natima Lang, an agent of the Cardassian Communication Service, becomes romantically involved with the bartender Quark, who is selling food illegally to the Bajorans. He in turn uses her personal access codes to steal money from the Cardassian government.
On Tyrus VII, Dr. Farallon begins work on the particle fountain project.
On Velara III, drilling operations begin at tapping the water in the subsurface layer.
Paul Menegay is born.

Prior to formally taking command of the starship, Captain Jean-Luc Picard picks his senior staff, including Geordi La Forge, Natasha Yar, and William T. Riker. He decides on Riker as first officer based on an incident on the USS Hood.
The dilithium crystal chamber that will be used on the Enterprise is designed on outpost Seran T-1. It is installed on the Enterprise at Utopia Planitia Fleet Yards.
The Enterprise begins its shakedown mission out of spacedock, before returning to dock at Earth Station McKinley.
The Enterprise is commissioned at the Utopia Planitia Fleet Yards, Mars.
The Enterprise is launched from the Utopia Planitia Fleet Yards.
The K.E. Tsiolkovsky – an Oberth Federation science vessel – is commissioned at the Baikonur Cosmodrome, USSR.
The Olympia, under the command of Captain Lisa Cusak, leaves the Alpha Quadrant on an eight year exploration of the Beta Quadrant.
The Phoenix is commissioned at the 40 Eridani A Starfleet Construction Yards.
The Vau N'Akat known as "The Diviner" arrives back in time from 2436 and begins his twenty-year long quest to find the USS Protostar.
Vaatrik marries Pallra.
Zek wins the Global Tongo Championship for the seventeenth year in a row.

A Ferengi historian collects a large amount of data concerning the origin of space travel in the Federation, intending to market it as a nostalgic gift item. One of the data items included in the Ferengi database is an image from 2050 of Shannon Janeway with her family.

A few weeks before the USS Enterprise-D visits the Vandor system, there is an accident in one of the labs of the research outpost on the fourth planet. There are two survivors: Dr. Paul Manheim and his wife, Jenice.
Aamin Marritza starts to work as an instructor at the Cardassian Military Academy on Kora II.
Captain Jean-Luc Picard is awarded a Service Award for his time aboard the USS Reliant.

First contact made after discovering the existence of the microbrains of Velara III, who had been attempting contact themselves. They request to be left alone for three centuries and would consider re-opening communications with the Federation in 2664.
For the first time, in what would be a pattern for the next six years, Jean-Luc Picard finds a way to avoid the annual Admirals' Banquet.
Lore is discovered on Omicron Theta by the Enterprise crew. He is reassembled, but later transported into space when he tries to betray the Enterprise to the Crystalline Entity.
Mardah leaves her foster family on Bajor to live on her own.
Morn visits Quark's for the first time.
Natasha Yar is killed on Vagra II by the entity known as Armus.
On Yadera II, the father of Taya dies.

Parasitic beings infiltrate Starfleet Command. Admirals Gregory Quinn and Norah Satie are instrumental in uncovering the alien plot, Dr. Beverly Crusher discovers a means of removing the parasites from the host, and Captain Picard and Commander Riker destroy the mother creature. The parasites transmit a homing message before they are killed.
Senator Pardek represents Romulus on a Barolian trade conference.

Several Federation and Romulan outposts close to the Neutral Zone are destroyed prior to and on stardate 41903.2. Both the Federation and the Romulans suspect each other as the cause. Based on similarities with other attacked stations, it is probable these events marked the first appearance of the Borg in Federation space during the 24th century.
Six months before the starship Enterprise-D visits the Omega Sagitta system, Benzan of Straleb and Yanar of Atlec begin courting each other with the assistance of Captain Thadiun Okona.
Sneed is arrested on Cardassia. He is charged with fraud and sentenced to 2 years labor on a Bajoran colony.
Starfleet Admiral Mark Jameson dies on Mordan IV.
Starfleet begins a search for a suitable star which will meet the requirements, including its similarity to the Kaelon II sun, for a test of the helium fusion enhancement.
Terok Nor security chief Odo is made an officer of the court in the Cardassian legal system in order to testify in criminal cases.
The Bajoran Ro Laren graduates from Starfleet Academy and is assigned to the USS Wellington.
The Enterprise-D travels to Ligon II to acquire a vaccine for Anchilles fever that afflicted the population of Styris IV from the Ligonians.
The Enterprise is hurled to the galaxy known as M-33 by the Traveler.

episodes.title: Where No One Has Gone Before

events.summary: The Enterprise makes first official contact with the Ferengi.

The Enterprise receives a strange message from the crew of SS Tsiolkovsky, an Oberth vessel observing a red supergiant that is about to explode into a white dwarf. Upon arrival, the crew is discovered all dead by an away team. The crew of the Tsiolkovsky was suffering from polywater intoxication, which is later introduced to the Enterprise crew by Geordi La Forge. The Enterprise is nearly destroyed by a stellar core fragment from the supergiant after Jim Shimoda removes all the isolinear chips from engineering, preventing the Enterprise from going to warp, but the ship is saved by the actions of Lieutenant Commander Data and Wesley Crusher.
The Jem'Hadar Virak'kara is created in the Gamma Quadrant.
The mythical planet of Aldea is revealed to exist.
The Romulan Star Empire returns to interstellar politics after fifty-three years of isolation.

The star Beta Magellan goes supernova. Computer systems on the planet Bynaus, which is close by, are destabilized. The Bynars temporarily hijack the Enterprise-D from Starbase 74 in order to repair their damaged computers.

The USS Enterprise-D, under the command of Captain Jean-Luc Picard, departs Earth on its first mission, to Farpoint Station. The official order to take command is signed by Rear Admiral Norah Satie on stardate 41148. The Enterprise subsequently makes first official contact with the Q entity and the lifeform which formed Farpoint. Admiral Leonard McCoy spends a day inspecting Enterprise’s medical layout.
The USS Stargazer, Picard's first command, is returned to Starfleet custody by the Ferengi.
William T. Riker is offered command of the ill-fated USS Drake, but instead accepts posting to the new USS Enterprise as first officer.
Zek wins the Global Tongo Championship for the eighteenth year in a row.
A 328-year-old mystery is solved when the Enterprise crew finds the remains of Colonel Stephen G. Richey, a NASA astronaut whose spacecraft, Charybdis, disappeared in 2037.
A month before stardate 42823.2, Starbase 73 receives a distress call from the Earth colony of Bringloid V.
A Talaxian ship with a crew of twenty-three is taken prisoner by the Vidiians and sent to Avery III.
Benjamin Sisko and Solok meet for what will be the last time for ten years.

episodes.title: Take Me Out to the Holosuite

events.summary: Calvin Hutchinson meets Beverly Crusher for the last time before they are see each again four years later on Arkaria.

Captain Jean-Luc Picard helps to define the rights of artificial life after Commander Bruce Maddox attempts to disassemble Data in order to construct more androids like him. Data is granted the full rights appropriate to all sentient lifeforms.
Captain L. Isao Telaka records his last log.
Chu'lak begins to serve aboard the USS Grissom.
Commander William T. Riker becomes the first Starfleet officer to serve on a Klingon vessel when he participates in the Officer Exchange Program.
Dr. Beverly Crusher leaves the Enterprise-D to become head of Starfleet Medical. She is replaced by Dr. Katherine Pulaski.
Dukat brings Odo to Terok Nor to investigate the murder of Vaatrik, a Bajoran chemist. Odo meets Kira Nerys and Quark for the first time, but does not solve the case.
Durango and Carol Freeman meet and begin a fifteen-year friendship.

Eight weeks before stardate 42494.8: The twenty-six crew members of the supply ship are examined by a medical staff. They are judged as fit for duty. The USS Lantree begins its tour of duty in Gamma 7 sector.
Five days before – The first officer is treated for Thelusian flu.
Geordi La Forge transfers to the Operations division, is promoted to full lieutenant and made the Enterprise chief engineer.
Guinan comes aboard the USS Enterprise at Captain Jean-Luc Picard's personal request. She takes the position of bartender in Ten Forward.
In a two hour lecture course in astral physics, Wesley Crusher first learns of the Elway Theorem.
In a winter flood on Mintaka III, six Mintakans drown. Counted among the dead, there are four children and two adults. One of the adults is the wife of Liko and mother to their daughter Oji.
Morn, Hain, Larell, Krit, and Nahsk take part in the Mother's Day Heist on Lissepia.
Odo begins trying to prove Quark is a thief.
One week before stardate 42494.8, Kingsley celebrates her 35th birthday on Gagarin IV.
Riker is offered command of the USS Aries, but declines the offer to remain with the Enterprise.
Starfleet initiates a series of tactical training and development programs designed to ready the fleet for the Borg invasion.
The Enterprise-D reunites the descendants of the colony ship SS Mariposa on the planet Mariposa.
The United Federation of Planets makes first contact with the Borg after Q hurls the Enterprise-D seven thousand light years to System J-25.
The USS Yamato is destroyed after discovering the homeworld of the ancient Iconians.
Three days before – The Lantree visits the Darwin Genetic Research Station on Gagarin IV.
Tuvok and Kathryn Janeway begin to form a bond as Janeway learns to rely on Tuvok's advice.
Two Ferengi try to break into Fort Knox around this year.
Worf transfers to the Operations division and is permanently made Enterprise security chief.
Zek wins the Global Tongo Championship for the nineteenth year in a row.
A peace treaty ends the Federation-Cardassian War.

A Romulan ship, which participated in the Khitomer Massacre of 2346, is captured by warriors of the Klingon Empire. Her logs identify the traitor at Khitomer as Ja'rod, the father of Klingon High Council member Duras.
Alexander Rozhenko, son of Worf, is born to K'Ehleyr.
Ambassador Sarek is diagnosed with Bendii Syndrome, shortly before he conducts final negotiations with the Legarans.
Anjohl Tennan dies in the Batal labor camp.
B'Elanna Torres enters Starfleet Academy. This is also the last time she talks to her mother, Miral, before being stranded in the Delta Quadrant.
Benjamin and Jennifer Sisko separate.
Benjamin Sisko wears his Starfleet dress uniform for the last time until 2369.
Crewmember Sandoval is killed after being hit by a disruptor blast. She lived for about a week after being injured.
D'Ghor begins to systematically attack the House of Kozak in order to financially weaken it.
Data creates another Soong-type android, known as Lal. Unfortunately, Lal's positronic net destabilizes and Data is forced to deactivate her.
Dr. Beverly Crusher returns to the USS Enterprise. Geordi La Forge is promoted to lieutenant commander, while Worf is promoted to full lieutenant.
Four months before stardate 43462, for openly criticizing the policies of the government of the Romulan Star Empire, Admiral Alidar Jarok is censured and reassigned to a remote sector.
Gwyndala is born.
Harry Kim enters Starfleet Academy.
In a holoprogram created by Barash, a research station was set up on Miridian VI.
Lore is rescued by a Pakled trade ship.
Lurak T'Luco is arrested in Vvalti, Romulus. He is found guilty of hijacking, theft, and assault and sentenced to five years imprisonment.
Nikolai Rozhenko and Worf see each other for the last time until 2370.
Odo is designated an officer of the Cardassian court in order to testify in criminal cases.
On Rutia IV, these events occurred prior to stardate 43510:
Rabal is convinced by his sister Serova’s research that warp drive was harming their homeworld Hekaras II.
Six months before stardate 43997, Commander Elizabeth Shelby takes over the Borg tactical analysis at Starfleet Tactical.
Sneed is released from prison. He had served two years for fraud.
Starfleet begins development of the USS Defiant.
Starfleet issues new type 2 phaser and tricorder designs. Starfleet uniforms are redesigned, now in two-piece versions with a higher collar and no striping on the shoulders.
Starfleet updates the uniform design, becoming a two-piece uniform, but keeping the same black shoulders/leggings and colored midriff/sleeves from the previous uniform design.

The Bajorans Ishan Chaye, Jillur Gueta, and Timor Landi are executed on Terok Nor after an assassination attempt on Gul Dukat. Three days later, another bombing leads Odo, who had been in charge of the investigation, to realize the three Bajorans were innocent.
The Cardassian ship Ravinok is attacked by the Breen and forced to crash land on Dozaria.

The Klingon High Council gives Worf a discommendation when his father, Mogh, is accused of aiding the Romulans at the Khitomer Massacre. However, the Enterprise crew discovers data which indicates that Duras' father, Ja'rod, was the traitor, and Worf accepts discommendation in order to keep the Klingon Empire from disintegrating into civil war.
The Romulan Admiral Alidar Jarok defects to the Federation, with faulty information about a new outpost on Nelvana III, designed to lure the Enterprise-D into the Neutral Zone.

The Romulan scout ship Pi crashes on Galorndon Core. It is suspected the ship was conducting covert operations in Federation space before it crashed. The Federation and the Romulans nearly go to war over the incident.
The soccer player Golanga has his knee replaced by a bio-implant.

The United Federation of Planets is attacked by a Borg cube, which annihilates the New Providence colony on Jouret IV, as well as the freighter USS Lalo. The Enterprise-D intercepts the Borg near the Paulson Nebula, and Captain Jean-Luc Picard is abducted and assimilated. The Enterprise prepares an energy weapon based on its deflector dish, but Picard, as Locutus of Borg, gives the Borg knowledge which renders the deflector weapon useless. The Borg continue on a course to Earth and destroy the Federation Fleet at Wolf 359 USS Saratoga is also lost during the battle. Data and Worf manage to rescue Picard and separate him from the collective destroying the cube in the process and saving Earth. (DS9: "Emissary") (TNG: "The Best of Both Worlds Part II")

The USS Enterprise emerges from a temporal rift. It temporarily remains in this year, causing an alternate timeline in which the Federation is fighting a losing war with the Klingon Empire. Captain Rachel Garrett is killed in a Klingon attack, and Lieutenant Richard Castillo is forced to take command. The ship returns to the year 2344 and the changes to the timeline are averted, although an alternate version of Natasha Yar returns to the past with the Enterprise-C.

Three days before stardate 43152, the Husnock attack and destroy the Federation colony on Rana IV. Among the dead is Rishon Uxbridge. In an act of revenge, her husband Kevin, who is actually an immortal and all-powerful Douwd, annihilates all fifty billion Husnock.
William T. Riker is offered command of the USS Melbourne.
Zek wins the Global Tongo Championship for the twentieth year in a row.

A number of individuals from both the ships at Wolf 359 and other vessels in the area are captured and assimilated by the Borg while en route to Sector 001. Some are returned to the Delta Quadrant by unknown means.

According to Montgomery Scott, while trapped in the mirror universe, Scott stated that the field density between the prime and mirror universes was closing rapidly and, should an escape not be made within the next thirty minutes, it would not be until this year in which another attempt could be made.
An eclipse occurs on Banea, in the Delta Quadrant. Lidell Ren tells Tom Paris four years later that this was a turn on for her husband Tolen, and that this was the last time "she was his wife".
Benjamin Sisko attends a Starfleet briefing on Q.

Chancellor K'mpec of the Klingon High Council dies, causing a power struggle between Duras and Gowron. Picard is appointed as Arbiter of Succession. Duras murders Ambassador K'Ehleyr after she discovers evidence of treachery and collusion with the Romulans by the House of Duras. Worf then claims right of vengeance against Duras, killing him.
Chief Miles O'Brien marries Keiko
Commander Elizabeth Shelby heads the task force to build starships in order to bolster the numbers lost to the Borg cube.

episodes.title: The Best of Both Worlds, Part II

events.summary: Haley is brought online at the Jupiter Station Holoprogramming Center by Lewis Zimmerman.

In another quantum reality, Picard is lost in an encounter with the Borg and Riker succeeds him as captain of the Enterprise-D with Worf as his first officer. In the same alternate reality, Worf and Deanna Troi begin a romantic relationship and have their first child, a son named Eric-Christopher.

Internal tensions in the Klingon Empire affect the Federation when it is suspected a Klingon exchange officer provided information about the Galaxy warp core to the Romulans, causing an explosion on board the Enterprise-D. Admiral Norah Satie is sent to investigate. The explosion is later traced back to the refit earlier in the year, but Satie initiates a "witch hunt," eventually implicating Captain Picard in a massive conspiracy.
Julian Bashir, Erit, and Elizabeth Lense attend a New Year's Eve party at Bruce Lucier's.
Melissa Willoughby is born.

episodes.title: Treachery, Faith and the Great River

events.summary: Peaceful relations are solidified between the Federation and the Cardassian Union, ending years of war and bloodshed between the two powers. The USS Phoenix, under the command of Captain Benjamin Maxwell, launches a series of illegal raids against the Cardassians, in hopes of instigating renewed hostilities. The Phoenix is tracked down by the Enterprise-D, with the assistance of Gul Macet, and eventually surrenders.
Q takes Vash on a two-year journey through the Gamma Quadrant.

Romulan Commander Sela orders the abduction of Lieutenant Commander Geordi La Forge and uses a brainwashing technique to instruct him to murder the Klingon governor of Krios, Vagh, and thus draw the Federation into conflict with the Klingons. The plan is thwarted, and further Romulan/Klingon cooperation uncovered.
(Stardate 44286.5) William T. Riker turns 32 years old and has his birthday party held in Ten Forward.
Telek R'Mor of the Romulan Astrophysical Academy dies before he can send messages he received from the stranded starship USS Voyager in the year 2371 in the Delta Quadrant.
The Borg implants are removed from Picard, and he returns to command of the Enterprise, although he requires several weeks of therapy. Riker returns to his post as first officer.

episodes.title: The Best of Both Worlds, Part II

events.summary: The Brunali homeworld is attacked by the Borg Collective.
The Enterprise docks at Earth Station McKinley for six weeks of repair work. While there, a defective dilithium chamber hatch is installed.
The Federation Archaeology Council holds its annual symposium aboard the Enterprise-D in orbit of Tagus III. Renowned names such as Switzer are in attendance.
The Juarez baby is born.

The Klingon Civil War begins as Gowron is installed as leader of the High Council. Gowron is opposed by Duras' sisters, Lursa and B'Etor, who are supported and supplied by the Romulans. Worf resigns his Starfleet commission to fight with Gowron, and his honor within the Klingon Empire is restored.
The Trill Jadzia is joined with the Dax symbiont; Curzon, the previous host, dies.

The USS Enterprise is severely damaged following an attempt to destroy a Borg cube and is unable to join the fleet massed at Wolf 359. William T. Riker is field promoted to captain of the Enterprise, and he chooses Borg specialist Commander Elizabeth Shelby as his first officer. At the Battle of Wolf 359, a total of thirty-nine Federation starships are destroyed by the Borg and eleven thousand lives are lost, including Benjamin Sisko's wife, Jennifer. Riker eventually devises a plan to recover Jean-Luc Picard, assimilated as Locutus of Borg, and the Enterprise crew use him to exploit the weakness of the Borg Collective hive mind. The cube is eventually destroyed in orbit of Earth.

episodes.title: The Best of Both Worlds, Part II

events.summary: The USS Enterprise is temporarily transferred near the center of the galaxy by Lieutenant Barclay, where an intelligent species known as the Cytherians is discovered.

This year marks the passage of the hundred years after which Spock suggested to Kirk that it would be interesting to return to Ceti Alpha V "and learn what crop had sprung from the seed you had planted".

episodes.title: Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan

events.summary: Thomas Riker made a phaser beam carving of the Janaran Falls in metal on Nervala IV. Two years later, he presents this carving as a gift to Deanna Troi.
Tova Veer becomes Forra Gegen's assistant.
Vulcan Ambassador T'Pel is discovered to be a Romulan spy. She is unwittingly returned to a Romulan vessel, with many Federation secrets, before the deception is uncovered.
Wesley Crusher leaves the Enterprise once he is accepted into Starfleet Academy.
Zek wins the Global Tongo Championship for the twenty-first year in a row.
Zio is imprisoned in the Akritirian maximum security detention facility.

A Borg scout ship crashes in the Argolis Cluster. The Enterprise retrieves a drone, Third of Five, from the crash site and intends to return it to the Borg Collective with a software weapon. The drone later develops a personality and is named Hugh. Picard does not implement the weapon but allows Hugh to return to the Borg.

A Borg vessel carrying Seven of Nine and several other Borg drones crashes on a planet on the Delta Quadrant. The drones are separated from the Borg Collective and start to regain their identities, but are relinked into a temporary collective established by Seven of Nine.

A peace treaty is established ending the centuries-long war between Krios Prime and Valt Minor. Despite Ambassador Briam's being incapacitated by injury, this treaty is made possible by the diplomatic assistance of Jean-Luc Picard and the dutiful self-sacrifice of the metamorph Kamala.

A probe from the long-dead planet Kataan telepathically transmits information about its culture, in the form of memories of a man named Kamin, into the brain of Captain Picard. Picard recovers a Ressikan flute from the probe, a relic from the entire lifetime he experienced while unconscious for merely twenty-five minutes.

Ambassador Spock is sighted on Romulus and the Federation Council worries that he has possibly defected. He is actually working with an underground movement that has the goal of unifying the Vulcan and Romulan peoples. Romulan officials, led by Sela, try to exploit the underground to send an invasion fleet to Vulcan, but Captain Picard, Lieutenant Commander Data, and Spock uncover the plot and the invasion forces are destroyed.
B'Elanna Torres drops out of Starfleet Academy.
Beckett Mariner enters Starfleet Academy as a first-year cadet.
Bok buys himself out of Rog Prison.
Captain Picard is able to open communication with the enigmatic Tamarians.
Commander Chakotay visits Starfleet Command for the last time before being marooned in the Delta Quadrant, and resigns his commission to Admiral Nimembeh.
Doctor April Wade is nominated for the Carrington Award.
Enabran Tain retires as head of the Obsidian Order and settles on the Arawath Colony.
Fesek's son is born on Malon Prime.
Gowron secludes himself and begins to rewrite Klingon history, excising any mention of the Enterprise’s help.
Hector Ilario enters Starfleet Academy.

In a second quantum reality, Worf and Troi began a romantic relationship while he was recovering from his spinal injury on Stardate 45587. Six months later, Worf formally asked Riker's permission to court Troi as he felt that not doing so would be dishonorable. The two eventually married.
In another quantum reality, Commander Worf, the first officer of the Enterprise-D, and his wife Deanna Troi had their second child, a girl named Shannara.
Julian Bashir, Elizabeth Lense, and Erit graduate from the Starfleet Medical Academy. Lense was valedictorian and Bashir was salutatorian.
Kira Nerys is fighting the Cardassians in a nameless swamp on Bajor.

episodes.title: In the Hands of the Prophets

events.summary: Lieutenant Aquiel Uhnari is assigned to Relay Station 47.

Lieutenant Commander Geordi La Forge develops a tachyon detection grid in order to detect cloaked ships and reveal the Romulan involvement. They are forced to withdraw, leaving the Duras without support and Gowron victorious.

Lieutenant Commander Geordi La Forge develops a tachyon detection grid in order to detect cloaked ships and reveal the Romulan involvement. They are forced to withdraw, leaving the Duras without support and Gowron victorious.
Mezoti is born.
Neelix visits a planetoid with rich dilithium signatures that will later be visited by the USS Voyager in 2371.

Nova Squadron, including Cadet Wesley Crusher, is involved in a deadly accident at the Saturn flight range. The members of the team were attempting a Kolvoord Starburst, a maneuver banned by Starfleet Academy because of its danger. The team covers up the accident, but evidence uncovered by Captain Picard convinces Wesley to come forward with the truth.
Richard Bashir tells his son he could do research on Earth instead of Deep Space 9.

Sometime this year, to cope with the stress of living aboard Bajoran space station Deep Space 9, Elim Garak activates the Obsidian Order cranial implant designed to help him resist torture and leaves it running continuously for the next two years.
Starfleet introduces the Danube runabout.
The celebrated ambassador Sarek of Vulcan dies at the age of 203 of complications from Bendii Syndrome.
The Enterprise attempts to communicate with the Crystalline Entity, but it is destroyed by Dr. Kila Marr.

The Enterprise is struck by a quantum filament and experiences heavy damage including a near-loss of antimatter containment. Molly O'Brien, daughter of Miles and Keiko O'Brien, is born in the ship's Ten Forward lounge during the crisis.
The first test of the soliton wave is conducted near Bilana III.

The Klingon Civil War between the Duras family and Gowron continues. Although Gowron's forces are outnumbered and on the verge of defeat, a Starfleet armada led by Jean-Luc Picard and the USS Enterprise blockades Romulan support of Duras.
The Ktarians attempt to take control of the Federation, but are thwarted by Enterprise-D personnel.
The Romulans experiment with interphase cloaking technology but fail to perfect it.
The SS Vico is destroyed in the black cluster. Its remains are towed back to Starbase 514 to be scrapped.

The USS Bozeman emerges from a temporal causality loop in which it was trapped for ninety years. In the process, it collides with the Enterprise-D and causes a temporal loop to form at the time of the collision. The loop repeats for seventeen days until the Enterprise crew discovers how to avoid the collision.
Tuvok receives his meditation lamp from a Vulcan master, paying twice the normal price.
Unknown at the time, Hugh's return causes widespread systems problems in the Borg, resulting in many drones simply being cut off from the Collective.
Zek wins the Global Tongo Championship for the twenty-second year in a row.
A back-up file is installed on the Cardassian ATR-4107 missile in this year, on stardate 46437.5.
A Cardassian sneak attack on the Federation system Minos Korva, staged in the McAllister C-5 Nebula, is prevented by Captain Edward Jellico, temporarily in command of the Enterprise-D.
A crucial victory is won for Spock's reunification efforts on Romulus when Vice-Proconsul M'ret defects to the Federation.

A duplicate of William T. Riker created due to a transporter accident, known as Thomas Riker, is rescued from the outpost on Nervala IV, where he was stranded for eight years. Thomas Riker is later transferred to the USS Gandhi.
A Jem'Hadar ship crashes on Bopak III. The only survivor is Goran'Agar, who unexpectedly survives for thirty-five days without ketracel-white.

Another variant of the Starfleet uniform is released, this time with the entire outfit black except for the division color on the shoulders, which is a reversed color scheme of the regular duty uniform and resembles the cadet uniforms with a purplish-gray undershirt, is added as well.
Arjin's father dies.
Benjamin Sisko meets General Krim for the first time, during a session of the Bajoran Executive Committee.
Captain Jean-Luc Picard is taken captive by Cardassian forces on Celtris III and tortured by Gul Madred. He is released after the embarrassing defeat of the Cardassians at the McAllister Nebula.
Captain Silva La Forge becomes the commanding officer of the USS Hera.
Data paints a pair of two oil paintings on canvas, one of which he titles Daughter, and gifts them to Jean-Luc Picard while onboard the Enterprise-D.
Ensign Ro Laren departs the Enterprise-D to participate in Starfleet's Advanced Tactical Training program.
Ferengi Pirate DaiMon Lurin hijacks the USS Enterprise-D.

Kai Opaka is left in the Gamma Quadrant on a moon occupied by two warring factions. Unable to leave the moon ever again, she hopes to ultimately end the fighting. Also, The United Federation of Planets is comprised of over one hundred planets as of this year.
Keiko O'Brien opens a school on Deep Space 9.
Kes is born on Ocampa.
Montgomery Scott is discovered to be alive, suspended in the transporter buffer of the USS Jenolan. The USS Enterprise discovers a Dyson sphere upon locating the Jenolan.
Neelix hangs a preserved Talaxian Furfly in the engine room of the Baxial.

On Deep Space 9, Chief Miles O'Brien unintentionally activates a long-dormant aphasia device while repairing the command level replicators. The device was planted there by Bajorans Dekon Elig and Surmak Ren when the Cardassians were in command of the station in 2351. An aphasia virus sweeps through DS9, threatening to eliminate the station's personnel. Fortunately, Major Kira Nerys is able to locate Dr. Surmak and he discovers an antidote.

On Earth, the quadricentennial of Apollo 11’s landing on the moon in 1969 is celebrated. Starfleet Academy gives out a certificate in relation to it, in some way. One of the recipients is Cadet Harry Kim.
Seska, an agent of the Obsidian Order, joins the Maquis, posing as a Bajoran.

Several weeks later, the Enterprise-D returned to Deep Space 9 to help repair the Bajoran aqueduct systems that had been damaged during the Occupation. Worf learns that survivors of the Khitomer Massacre, including his father, may have been captured and imprisoned by the Romulans in the Carraya system by the Yridian information dealer Jaglom Shrek. He discovers the camp, and while his father is not among the survivors, there are indeed captured Klingons, now living in peace with their Romulan captors. Several younger members of the camp decide to leave with Worf and are sworn to secrecy about the existence of the camp in order to preserve the honor of families back in the Klingon Empire. Meanwhile on the Enterprise, Data experiences a serious of visions due to an accident while helping DS9 Chief Medical Officer Julian Bashir. Data discovers that these visions are dreams. The dreams are those of his father: the late Doctor Noonien Soong.

Shortly afterwards, Commander Benjamin Sisko and Lieutenant Jadzia Dax discover the Bajoran wormhole, the first stable wormhole known to exist. Sisko makes direct contact with the Prophets, becoming the Emissary of the Prophets as prophesied in ancient Bajoran mythology.
The Cardassian Union ends its occupation of the planet Bajor, which lasted fifty years. As a result, the Cardassian Velos VII internment camp is closed on stardate 46302.

The clerics on Boreth create a clone of Kahless the Unforgettable, hoping to use him to reunite the Klingon people. Worf later uncovers the fact that this Kahless is a clone, and the clone is installed as a ceremonial leader of the Klingon people.

The crew of the Enterprise-D, along with Romulan, Klingon, and Cardassian interests, deciphers the last work of Professor Galen, revealing that a race of ancient humanoids is a common ancestor of most species in the Milky Way Galaxy.

The mining station Terok Nor in orbit of Bajor is abandoned during the Cardassian retreat. The Bajoran Provisional Government requests Starfleet assistance in maintaining the station, and it is renamed Deep Space 9. The Enterprise-D arrives to offload personnel and equipment.
The teachers on the ship agree not to hold Captain Picard Day in 2370, according to Jean-Luc Picard, a promise they will break.
The Vulcan Isolationist Movement hires mercenaries to find the remaining two pieces of the Stone of Gol.
The Vulcan Isolationist Movement steals a piece of the Stone of Gol from a Vulcan museum.
Toman'torax becomes Omet'iklan's Second.
Vash arrives at Deep Space 9 after two years in the Gamma Quadrant in the company of Q.
Zek wins the Global Tongo Championship for the twenty-third year in a row.
A group of Federation colony worlds declares an unofficial war on the Cardassian Union after rejecting the Federation-Cardassian Treaty. They call themselves the Maquis.

A second Borg incursion into Federation space continues (having begun in 2369, around stardate 46982.1), as individualized Borg drones are led by Lore, a Soong-type android. However, their attempt to annihilate the Federation is once again foiled by Captain Jean-Luc Picard and the USS Enterprise. Lore is defeated, deactivated, and dismantled.

Aboard the USS Prometheus, Gideon Seyetik makes an attempt to reignite Epsilon 119. To free his wife, Nidell, from their marriage, he pilots a shuttlecraft into the star, killing himself, but bringing Epsilon 119 back to life.
After Elim Garak becomes ill after overuse of a cranial implant, Julian Bashir receives help from Enabran Tain to save his friend's life.
Although the rescue of the Siskos, Nog, and Quark is successful, the Galaxy starship USS Odyssey is destroyed by a Jem'Hadar suicide attack.
Alyssa Ogawa marries Andrew Powell. The couple also conceived a child.
Alyssa Ogawa, Sam Lavelle, and Taurik are promoted to lieutenant junior grade.
An emergent lifeform uses the systems on board the USS Enterprise-D to reproduce, temporarily giving the Enterprise sentience.
B'Elanna Torres gets inside a Cardassian missile intended to destroy Maquis targets and reprograms it. She nicknames it Dreadnought.
B'Elanna Torres joins the Maquis after Chakotay saves her life.
Borum gives Rionoj Li Nalas' earring to smuggle out of the Hutet labor camp. Rionoj travels to Deep Space 9 and passes the earring to Quark, who later gives it to Kira Nerys.
By supporting a Bajoran extremist group called The Circle, the Cardassian Union tries to regain its influence on Bajor.
Cadet Harry Kim receives the Cochrane Medal of Excellence.
Captain Jean-Luc Picard successfully passes a test of Humanity's worth issued by the Q Continuum and prevents the destruction of the Human race by a temporal anomaly.
Deanna Troi is promoted to full commander and becomes a bridge officer upon passing the Bridge Officer's test.
Dr. Beverly Crusher discovers Barclay's Protomorphosis Syndrome when she inadvertently causes the Enterprise-D to become infected.
Edgar Willoughby, Jr. is born.

episodes.title: Treachery, Faith and the Great River

events.summary: Elaysian Ensign Melora Pazlar is transferred to Deep Space 9. Julian Bashir falls in love with her and plans a risky procedure based on the research of Nathaniel Teros to help her walk in normal gravity.
Ensign Sito Jaxa is believed killed during a mission to return a Cardassian officer working as a Federation spy to the Cardassian Union.
Harry Kim graduates from Starfleet Academy. He is "commissioned to rank of ensign."
Harry Kim's application to Voyager is denied.
In order to keep Opaka's role in the Kendra Valley Massacre secret, Bareil Antos withdraws from the kai election. Winn Adami is elected unopposed.
Jadzia Dax is reunited with Kang, Kor, and Koloth and joins them on a mission to kill an enemy who has eluded them for almost a century – The Albino.
Kathryn Janeway is chosen to command the USS Voyager.
Kim applies for service aboard Voyager. His application is approved.
Kim requests transfer to Starfleet Engineering Corps.
Kira Nerys and Julian Bashir are transported to the mirror universe.
Lorit Akrem dies in a mine collapse on Dozaria.

Miles O'Brien is captured and put on trial by the Cardassians, who claim he is a member of the Maquis. In reality, the Cardassians hoped to discredit the Federation and allow the Central Command to send ships into the DMZ and wipe out the Maquis.
Nog steals a springball racket from Major Kira Nerys' quarters.
Quark is executed. The primary Doctor Bashir kills Odo.
Ro Laren completes the Advanced Tactical Training program and is promoted to lieutenant. She returns to the Enterprise-D, but later defects to the Maquis.
Sisko takes Jake, Nog, and Quark to the Gamma Quadrant so that the two students can perform a planetary survey. Sisko and Quark are taken prisoner by the Jem'Hadar.
Sneed is arrested in Federation territory. He is found guilty of smuggling and sentenced to four years imprisonment.
Standard warp drive is discovered to have detrimental effects on the fabric of subspace. A program is launched to develop a safer means of warp travel, which is first incorporated on the Intrepid.
The Brunali homeworld is attacked by the Borg Collective.
The El-Aurian Martus Mazur sets up Club Martus on Deep Space 9's Promenade.

The Enterprise-D receives a new warp core on Thanatos VII, manufactured with interphasic techniques. The core also brings along interphasic organisms which attach themselves to the crew, but a method is later devised to remove them.
The Skrreea arrive from the Gamma Quadrant looking for the mythical planet Kentanna.
The Terran Rebellion begins.
The USS Equinox, commanded by Captain Rudolph Ransom, is commissioned.

The USS Pegasus is found in the Devolin system. The phasing cloak is retrieved by Admiral Erik Pressman and installed aboard the Enterprise-D to allow it to escape from within an asteroid. Illegal Starfleet involvement in the development and testing of the device is revealed.

Verad boards Deep Space 9, while the station is being operated by a skeleton crew, to steal the Dax symbiont from Jadzia Dax. He is later stopped by Benjamin Sisko and the symbiont is returned to Jadzia.
Wesley Crusher leaves Starfleet Academy to join the Traveler in explorations of alternate planes of reality.

While searching for suitable planets to establish colonies near the wormhole, Benjamin Sisko and Miles O'Brien discover a lost Erewon-class colony ship, and the remains of the colonists who have been living under an artificially created EM field for ten years. The leaders of the colony, Alixus and her son Vinod, who set up the field, are taken back to stand trial for the deaths of the colonists that could have been saved over the years.
William T. Riker and Deanna Troi observe the final adventure of the original Enterprise.
Zar begins his association with Dala, in which he will never have known her to perform menial labor for six years.
Zek wins the Global Tongo Championship for the twenty-fourth year in a row.
Zim Brott begins to serve aboard Deep Space 9.
According to, the fusion reactor in the Uxbridge house would last until this year.
Benjamin Sisko is killed by the Klingon-Cardassian Alliance. Miles "Smiley" O'Brien recruits the prime universe Benjamin Sisko to take his counterparts place temporarily.
Benjamin Sisko is promoted by Starfleet Command from commander to captain.
Captain James T. Kirk emerges from the Nexus and is killed helping Jean-Luc Picard prevent Dr. Soran from destroying the Veridian system.
Deep Space 9 is spared destruction by mere seconds after a legacy counter-insurgency program installed by the station's original Cardassian commander is inadvertently initiated.
Dr. Tolian Soran uses a trilithium weapon: the solar probe to collapse the Amargosa star.
Ensign Samantha Wildman discovers she is pregnant while stranded on Voyager in the Delta Quadrant.
It is discovered that the Founders of the Dominion have infiltrated the Alpha Quadrant.
Jennifer Sisko defects to the Terran Rebellion.
Kar, a young Kazon-Ogla, begins his combat training on Tarok.

Kes travels to this year from five years in the original version of the future. She attempts to give the crew of Voyager to the Vidiians in exchange for safe passage for her future and present self to Ocampa. Her plan fails and Janeway is forced to kill Kes. Kes then makes a holographic projection to prevent her future self from going back in time, thus changing history.
Kira Nerys speaks to Latha Mabrin for what will ultimately be the last time.
Lurak T'Luco is released from prison. He had served five years for hijacking, theft, and assault.
Marina Derex's Labyrinths of the Mind is published.
Martok is captured by the Dominion on Kang's Summit and sent to Internment Camp 371.
Neelix and Kes join the Voyager crew.

Originally, Voyager discovered the class M planet devastated by an explosion that happened the previous day due to the detonation of polaric ion energy. Captain Janeway and Tom Paris were thrown back in time before the explosion by a subspace fracture. The Voyager crew attempts to rescue them by opening a fracture with a polaric generator but cause the original explosion and destroy all life on the planet, including the captain. Janeway resets time by closing the fracture before it causes the explosion. This creates a new timeline in which Voyager never went to the planet and the civilization is spared.
Quark visits his family home for the first time in twenty years.
Reginald Barclay transfers to Jupiter Station and works at Dr. Lewis Zimmerman's holographic laboratory to test the Emergency Medical Hologram's interpersonal skills.
Robert Picard and his son René die in a fire at their home in La Barre, France.
Seska's true roots as a Cardassian spy are revealed. She betrays the Voyager crew and joins the Kazon.
Several crewmembers on Voyager, including first officer Cavit, flight control officer Stadi, the chief medical officer, and the chief engineer, are killed during the transit to the Delta Quadrant.
Smiley steals the design schematics for the USS Defiant during his visit to Deep Space 9.
Sometime around this year, variable geometry pylons are introduced on Federation starships.
Starfleet introduces a new combadge, a gold rectangle with a cutout oval in the center replacing the gold oval.
Steve Levy is promoted to Lieutenant.
The Bajoran Shakaar Edon, a former cell leader in the Bajoran Resistance, becomes first minister of Bajor's provisional government.

The Cardassian Obsidian Order, together with the Romulan Tal Shiar, are lured into a trap when they attack the Founders' homeworld in the Gamma Quadrant. The following battle leads to the complete destruction of the combined Romulan-Cardassian fleet of twenty ships and severely cripples both organizations, which, in turn, reduces possible resistance against the Dominion down to the Federation and the Klingon Empire.

The Enterprise is destroyed over Veridian III. Almost the entire crew survives in the vessel's saucer section after the ship's separation, which crash lands on the planet along with the attached captain's yacht Calypso after the ship's stardrive section exploded, impacting the saucer with an ion shock wave.

The Federation, as well as the other Alpha and Beta Quadrant powers, becomes aware of the Dominion threat after the USS Defiant returns from its mission to the Founders' homeworld in the Gamma Quadrant, the home of the designated leaders of the Dominion.
The Maquis member Thomas Riker steals the USS Defiant and uses it to expose a Cardassian military buildup in the Orias system.
The Obsidian Order exposes Legate Tekeny Ghemor's ties to the Cardassian dissident movement.

The Romulan Star Empire sends Ruwon and Karina to Deep Space 9 to take possession of Starfleet's data on the Dominion. This was agreed upon earlier in the year, in exchange for allowing the USS Defiant to operate a cloaking device. The Romulans also send a Warbird, intending to destroy the station and the wormhole. However, their plan is thwarted by Miles O'Brien, who had foreseen the future and warned Benjamin Sisko.

The starship USS Equinox disappears, pulled into the Delta Quadrant by the Caretaker. The ship sets a return course to the Alpha Quadrant, but encounters the Krowtonan Guard, becoming severely damaged while losing half of its crew.

The starship USS Voyager departs Deep Space 9 on a mission to find the Maquis raider Val Jean. Both ships are thrown seventy-five thousand light years into the Delta Quadrant by the Caretaker. The Caretaker's array, the only means home, is later destroyed by Captain Kathryn Janeway in order to protect the Ocampa and the two crews are forced to work together to find a route back to the Alpha Quadrant.
The USS Enterprise undergoes a major refit.

The USS Olympia crashes on a planet in Rutharian sector. Captain Lisa Cusak's distress call is sent three years into the future by the planet's energy barrier and is intercepted by the USS Defiant. Captain Cusak dies on the planet.
Tuvok joins Chakotay's Maquis cell as an undercover operative of Starfleet.

Voyager approaches an M-class planet. Kes enters the bridge, reporting a vision of a planet being destroyed. She is relieved to find out that the planet is teeming with life. It appeared to be a pre-warp civilization, so Captain Janeway makes a note in the log and orders Voyager to continue towards the Alpha Quadrant.

Voyager discovers a micro-wormhole. The crew sends in a microprobe, which reveals the wormhole leads to the Alpha Quadrant. Voyager, using the probe as a relay for a message, contacts a Romulan science vessel named Talvath, commanded by Telek R'Mor. However, it is later found out that the wormhole is a rift through time and that R'Mor is from the year 2351. After some consideration, R'Mor agrees to take the crew's personal messages with him and deliver them to Starfleet at the appropriate time. R'Mor is later discovered to have died in 2367 and the messages were apparently never delivered to Starfleet.
Voyager lands on a planet in the Delta Quadrant and discovers a colony of Humans, including Amelia Earhart, transported to the planet by the Briori centuries prior.
Voyager makes first contact with the Ocampa, Talaxians, and Kazon.
Voyager’s Tom Paris is convicted of murdering the Banean Tolen Ren. After much evidence gathering, Tuvok is able to prove Paris' innocence and reveals a Numiri plot to steal Ren's technology.
With help from Bareil Antos, Kai Winn and Legate Turrel sign the Bajoran-Cardassian Treaty. It sends political shock waves throughout the Alpha Quadrant. Bareil dies soon after.
Worf is promoted to lieutenant commander.
Zek wins the Global Tongo Championship for the twenty-fifth year in a row.
A Cardassian missile reprogrammed by B'Elanna Torres is found by Voyager in the Delta Quadrant, set for a course to destroy a planet. The missile is destroyed by Torres before it strikes the planet.

A member of the Q Continuum, Quinn, is released from his confinement in a comet by the crew of Voyager. After a brief trial, he relinquishes his powers so he can become Human and commit suicide. Q secures the poison used in the suicide. The event causes a civil war in the Continuum.
A transporter accident combines Tuvok and Neelix into a single being, Tuvix. He is later separated into his two halves against his will by Captain Janeway.

Admiral Leyton stages a military coup by taking advantage of the current Changeling scare. The attempt is thwarted by Benjamin Sisko and Odo. Leyton resigns his commission, and Sisko and Odo return to Deep Space 9.
After being convicted of espionage, Miles O'Brien is given the memories of a twenty-year prison sentence on Argratha.
An Annari warship commanded by General Valen becomes trapped in The Void.

Captain Benjamin Sisko takes the USS Defiant to the Founders' homeworld, hoping that the Founders will be able to cure Odo. It is revealed that the Founders had Weyoun infect Odo with the disease, necessitating his return. As punishment for killing a Changeling one year earlier, he is turned into a solid.

Captain Janeway and Commander Chakotay contract a virus from an insect bite and are forced to be left behind on the planet upon which they were infected. Tuvok becomes Voyager’s new commanding officer for six weeks until a cure is obtained from the Vidiians.

Captain Kathryn Janeway attempts to form an alliance with various Kazon sects to bring a stop to their increasingly frequent attacks on Voyager. Unfortunately, the negotiations are put to a stop after the Trabe attempt to wipe out the Kazon delegates.

Commander Chakotay discovers the Sky Spirits living on a planet in the Delta Quadrant, who visited Earth some forty-five thousand years before and reveals himself to be a descendant of the Inheritors.

Commander Chakotay is taken prisoner by the Kazon-Ogla after drifting into their space in a shuttlecraft while performing the pakra ceremony. He and a young Kazon boy named Kar escape a Kazon carrier vessel to the moon Tarok. Afterward, Kar earns his name after killing Jal Razik and Chakotay completes the pakra ceremony on the USS Voyager.

Convinced by a Changeling posing as General Martok that the Cardassian government had been infiltrated by the Founders, the Klingon Empire invades the Cardassian Union. However, the United Federation of Planets objects to this action, causing Chancellor Gowron to withdraw from the Khitomer Accords, ending their alliance. At the end of this year, the Changeling posing as General Martok convinces Gowron to take further aggressive action, causing the Klingon Empire to annex the Archanis sector from the Federation, thereby starting an open war between the two powers.

Doctor Julian Bashir is able to prevent unborn children of the people living on Boranis III from contracting a plague sent by the Dominion as punishment for the rejection of their offer of membership.
Greta Vanderweg is transferred to Deep Space 9.
Hector Ilario graduates from Starfleet Academy.
Jennifer Sisko is killed.
Kira Nerys begins a relationship with Shakaar Edon.
Kurn is given a new identity after the dishonor of the House of Mogh.

Michael Jonas begins secretly feeding the Kazon-Nistrim information about Voyager. Lieutenant Tuvok discovers the transmissions, but is unable to determine who sent them. To flush out the spy, Captain Janeway and Tuvok create a plan to have Tom Paris begin behaving like a malcontent to have a plausible reason for him to leave Voyager, be captured by Kazon forces, and extract information exposing the traitor.
Michael Jonas is revealed to be the traitor on Voyager supplying information to the Kazon. He is killed in a fight with Neelix.
Miles O'Brien and Julian Bashir are captured by a group of Jem'Hadar. Goran'Agar, their leader, orders Bashir to work on a cure for his men's addiction to ketracel-white.
Regent Worf attempts to retake Terok Nor from the rebels, but is defeated in battle by the newly-completed Defiant, commanded by Captain Sisko of the prime reality.
Starfleet detects unusual activity along the Romulan Neutral Zone.
Starfleet introduces styles of phasers and tricorders in this year.

The Antwerp Conference is bombed. An investigation reveals that the bomb was placed by a Changeling. Benjamin Sisko is temporarily reassigned to Earth to serve as Chief of Starfleet Security, in charge of preparing the Federation for a possible Dominion invasion.
The Bajoran wormhole undergoes an inversion that occurs once every fifty years.
The Doctor begins a brief relationship with the Vidiian Danara Pel.

The Kazon-Nistrim briefly steal transporter technology from Voyager. Chakotay is able to eliminate the Kazon's newfound technology and return to Voyager, but not before Seska informs him that she stole a sample of his DNA and impregnated herself with it.
The Klingon Empire attacks three Romulan outposts along the border.
The Korma Conference was scheduled to take place this year, however, the outpost hosting the conference was destroyed by a Klingon Bird-of-Prey that was later commandeered by Gul Dukat.
The Nanut was expected to end its two year mission to the Gamma Quadrant in this year.
The Terran Rebellion captures Terok Nor and begins constructing the ISS Defiant with the plans stolen from Deep Space 9 the previous year.
The USS Enterprise is launched from the San Francisco Fleet Yards on stardate 49827.5.
The war between the Druoda and an unnamed species in the Delta Quadrant ends.

Violating the rules of Ferengi society, Quark's employees, led by Rom, form a labor union and go on strike. Quark secretly agrees to the union's demands to protect his brother, and the union is dissolved. Rom quits working for Quark and takes a position under Chief Miles O'Brien on Deep Space 9's engineering crew.

Voyager discovers a new type of dilithium which allows a shuttlecraft, piloted by Tom Paris, to achieve warp 10 flight. However, the effects of this type of transwarp are proved detrimental to humanoid DNA, and it is abandoned.

Voyager encounters a plasma drift which somehow duplicates every single molecule on board the ship. Severe damage is inflicted to one Voyager, and Harry Kim dies after being blown out through a hull breach. Naomi Wildman is born to Samantha Wildman during the incident. The other Voyager is later attacked by Vidiians, but is able to send Kim along with the Wildman child, who survived on this vessel, to the damaged Voyager. The other Voyager then self-destructs.
Voyager encounters the Caretaker's mate, Suspiria, and discovers that she is hostile.
Voyager is taken by the Kazon-Nistrim, assisted by Seska, and the crew is marooned on Hanon IV.

While escorting a medical convoy to Pentath III, Lieutenant Commander Worf accidentally destroys a Klingon civilian convoy. The Klingon Empire tries to have Worf extradited and charged for the deaths of the 441 civilians on board. Fortunately, it is revealed that the transport was empty and giving false readings to justify an armed conflict, and the casualty list was in fact a list of the victims of an accidental transport crash that occurred three months before this incident.
Zek wins the Global Tongo Championship for the twenty-sixth year in a row.
A Deep Space 9 salvage team led by Miles O'Brien visits Empok Nor.
A group of Talaxians lead by Oxilon establish an asteroid colony.
A Starfleet team lead by Benjamin Sisko are successful in capturing a Jem'Hadar attack ship.
After the real identity of the Klingon General Martok as a Changeling is revealed, the war between the Federation and the Klingon Empire is ended with a temporary cease fire.
B'Elanna Torres begins to have dreams that turn out to be memories implanted by an elderly Enaran woman.
General Martok is given command of the IKS Rotarran and is successful in rescuing survivors of the IKS B'Moth.
In the Delta Quadrant, the crew of Voyager discover a race called the Voth, a species that evolved on Earth and migrated to the Delta Quadrant several million years ago.
Jake Sisko is successful in obtaining a Willie Mays baseball card for his father Benjamin.
Julian Bashir is replaced by a Changeling.
Keiko O'Brien is inhabited by a Pah-wraith who tries to kill the Prophets, but is stopped by Miles O'Brien. The entity is expelled from Keiko's body.
Kirayoshi O'Brien, the second child of Miles and Keiko O'Brien, is born.
Klingon Chancellor Gowron reinstates the Khitomer Accords with the Federation.

Lieutenant Tuvok began suffering from a memory virus accidentally transferred to his mind from Dimitri Valtane eighty years previously, in 2293. With Kathryn Janeway acting as his pyllora, the two try to discover the cause of the virus and inadvertently experience memories of Tuvok's first Starfleet posting – the USS Excelsior under the command of Captain Hikaru Sulu.
Quark and Odo get stranded on a remote class L planet and must work together to survive and get back to Deep Space 9.
Silaran Prin begins his revenge on the Shakaar resistance cell. He kills the majority of its former members but is stopped by Kira Nerys.
Starfleet Command declares Voyager officially lost with all hands. Kathryn Janeway begins writing letters to her family and fiancé.
